title: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag
text:
CWE-1004 Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag
The product uses a cookie to store sensitive information, but the cookie is not marked with the HttpOnly flag.

metadata.cwe_name: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag

title: CWE-1007: Insufficient Visual Distinction of Homoglyphs Presented to User
text:
CWE-1007 Insufficient Visual Distinction of Homoglyphs Presented to User
The product displays information or identifiers to a user, but the display mechanism does not make it easy for the user to distinguish between visually similar or identical glyphs (homoglyphs), which may cause the user to misinterpret a glyph and perform an unintended, insecure action.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Visual Distinction of Homoglyphs Presented to User

title: CWE-102: Struts: Duplicate Validation Forms
text:
CWE-102 Struts: Duplicate Validation Forms
The product uses multiple validation forms with the same name, which might cause the Struts Validator to validate a form that the programmer does not expect.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Duplicate Validation Forms

title: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames
text:
CWE-1021 Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames
The web application does not restrict or incorrectly restricts frame objects or UI layers that belong to another application or domain.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

title: CWE-1022: Use of Web Link to Untrusted Target with window.opener Access
text:
CWE-1022 Use of Web Link to Untrusted Target with window.opener Access
The web application produces links to untrusted external sites outside of its sphere of control, but it does not properly prevent the external site from modifying security-critical properties of the window.opener object, such as the location property.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Web Link to Untrusted Target with window.opener Access

title: CWE-1023: Incomplete Comparison with Missing Factors
text:
CWE-1023 Incomplete Comparison with Missing Factors
The product performs a comparison between entities that must consider multiple factors or characteristics of each entity, but the comparison does not include one or more of these factors.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Comparison with Missing Factors

title: CWE-1024: Comparison of Incompatible Types
text:
CWE-1024 Comparison of Incompatible Types
The product performs a comparison between two entities, but the entities are of different, incompatible types that cannot be guaranteed to provide correct results when they are directly compared.

metadata.cwe_name: Comparison of Incompatible Types

title: CWE-1025: Comparison Using Wrong Factors
text:
CWE-1025 Comparison Using Wrong Factors
The code performs a comparison between two entities, but the comparison examines the wrong factors or characteristics of the entities, which can lead to incorrect results and resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Comparison Using Wrong Factors

title: CWE-103: Struts: Incomplete validate() Method Definition
text:
CWE-103 Struts: Incomplete validate() Method Definition
The product has a validator form that either does not define a validate() method, or defines a validate() method but does not call super.validate().

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Incomplete validate() Method Definition

title: CWE-1037: Processor Optimization Removal or Modification of Security-critical Code
text:
CWE-1037 Processor Optimization Removal or Modification of Security-critical Code
The developer builds a security-critical protection mechanism into the software, but the processor optimizes the execution of the program such that the mechanism is removed or modified.

metadata.cwe_name: Processor Optimization Removal or Modification of Security-critical Code

title: CWE-1038: Insecure Automated Optimizations
text:
CWE-1038 Insecure Automated Optimizations
The product uses a mechanism that automatically optimizes code, e.g. to improve a characteristic such as performance, but the optimizations can have an unintended side effect that might violate an intended security assumption.

metadata.cwe_name: Insecure Automated Optimizations

title: CWE-1039: Inadequate Detection or Handling of Adversarial Input Perturbations in Automated Recognition Mechanism
text:
CWE-1039 Inadequate Detection or Handling of Adversarial Input Perturbations in Automated Recognition Mechanism
The product uses an automated mechanism such as machine learning to recognize complex data inputs (e.g. image or audio) as a particular concept or category, but it does not properly detect or handle inputs that have been modified or constructed in a way that causes the mechanism to detect a different, incorrect concept.

metadata.cwe_name: Inadequate Detection or Handling of Adversarial Input Perturbations in Automated Recognition Mechanism

title: CWE-104: Struts: Form Bean Does Not Extend Validation Class
text:
CWE-104 Struts: Form Bean Does Not Extend Validation Class
If a form bean does not extend an ActionForm subclass of the Validator framework, it can expose the application to other weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Form Bean Does Not Extend Validation Class

title: CWE-1041: Use of Redundant Code
text:
CWE-1041 Use of Redundant Code
The product has multiple functions, methods, procedures, macros, etc. that
 contain the same code.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Redundant Code

title: CWE-1042: Static Member Data Element outside of a Singleton Class Element
text:
CWE-1042 Static Member Data Element outside of a Singleton Class Element
The code contains a member element that is declared as static (but not final), in which
 its parent class element 
 is not a singleton class - that is, a class element that can be used only once in
 the 'to' association of a Create action.

metadata.cwe_name: Static Member Data Element outside of a Singleton Class Element

title: CWE-1043: Data Element Aggregating an Excessively Large Number of Non-Primitive Elements
text:
CWE-1043 Data Element Aggregating an Excessively Large Number of Non-Primitive Elements
The product uses a data element that has an excessively large
 number of sub-elements with non-primitive data types such as structures or aggregated objects.

metadata.cwe_name: Data Element Aggregating an Excessively Large Number of Non-Primitive Elements

title: CWE-1044: Architecture with Number of Horizontal Layers Outside of Expected Range
text:
CWE-1044 Architecture with Number of Horizontal Layers Outside of Expected Range
The product's architecture contains too many - or too few -
 horizontal layers.

metadata.cwe_name: Architecture with Number of Horizontal Layers Outside of Expected Range

title: CWE-1045: Parent Class with a Virtual Destructor and a Child Class without a Virtual Destructor
text:
CWE-1045 Parent Class with a Virtual Destructor and a Child Class without a Virtual Destructor
A parent class has a virtual destructor method, but the parent has a child class that does not have a virtual destructor.

metadata.cwe_name: Parent Class with a Virtual Destructor and a Child Class without a Virtual Destructor

title: CWE-1046: Creation of Immutable Text Using String Concatenation
text:
CWE-1046 Creation of Immutable Text Using String Concatenation
The product creates an immutable text string using string concatenation operations.

metadata.cwe_name: Creation of Immutable Text Using String Concatenation

title: CWE-1047: Modules with Circular Dependencies
text:
CWE-1047 Modules with Circular Dependencies
The product contains modules in which one module has references that cycle back to itself, i.e., there are circular dependencies.

metadata.cwe_name: Modules with Circular Dependencies

title: CWE-1048: Invokable Control Element with Large Number of Outward Calls
text:
CWE-1048 Invokable Control Element with Large Number of Outward Calls
The code contains callable control elements that
 contain an excessively large number of references to other
 application objects external to the context of the callable,
 i.e. a Fan-Out value that is excessively large.

metadata.cwe_name: Invokable Control Element with Large Number of Outward Calls

title: CWE-1049: Excessive Data Query Operations in a Large Data Table
text:
CWE-1049 Excessive Data Query Operations in a Large Data Table
The product performs a data query with a large number of joins
 and sub-queries on a large data table.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Data Query Operations in a Large Data Table

title: CWE-105: Struts: Form Field Without Validator
text:
CWE-105 Struts: Form Field Without Validator
The product has a form field that is not validated by a corresponding validation form, which can introduce other weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Form Field Without Validator

title: CWE-1050: Excessive Platform Resource Consumption within a Loop
text:
CWE-1050 Excessive Platform Resource Consumption within a Loop
The product has a loop body or loop condition that contains a control element that directly or
 indirectly consumes platform resources, e.g. messaging, sessions, locks, or file
 descriptors.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Platform Resource Consumption within a Loop

title: CWE-1051: Initialization with Hard-Coded Network Resource Configuration Data
text:
CWE-1051 Initialization with Hard-Coded Network Resource Configuration Data
The product initializes data using hard-coded values that act as network resource identifiers.

metadata.cwe_name: Initialization with Hard-Coded Network Resource Configuration Data

title: CWE-1052: Excessive Use of Hard-Coded Literals in Initialization
text:
CWE-1052 Excessive Use of Hard-Coded Literals in Initialization
The product initializes a data element using a hard-coded
 literal that is not a simple integer or static constant element.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Use of Hard-Coded Literals in Initialization

title: CWE-1053: Missing Documentation for Design
text:
CWE-1053 Missing Documentation for Design
The product does not have documentation that represents how it is designed.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Documentation for Design

title: CWE-1054: Invocation of a Control Element at an Unnecessarily Deep Horizontal Layer
text:
CWE-1054 Invocation of a Control Element at an Unnecessarily Deep Horizontal Layer
The code at one architectural layer invokes code that resides
 at a deeper layer than the adjacent layer, i.e., the invocation skips at least one
 layer, and the invoked code is not part of a vertical utility layer that can be referenced from any horizontal layer.

metadata.cwe_name: Invocation of a Control Element at an Unnecessarily Deep Horizontal Layer

title: CWE-1055: Multiple Inheritance from Concrete Classes
text:
CWE-1055 Multiple Inheritance from Concrete Classes
The product contains a class with inheritance from more than
 one concrete class.

metadata.cwe_name: Multiple Inheritance from Concrete Classes

title: CWE-1056: Invokable Control Element with Variadic Parameters
text:
CWE-1056 Invokable Control Element with Variadic Parameters
A named-callable or method control element has a signature that
 supports a variable (variadic) number of parameters or arguments.

metadata.cwe_name: Invokable Control Element with Variadic Parameters

title: CWE-1057: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component
text:
CWE-1057 Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component
The product uses a dedicated, central data manager component as required by design, but it contains code that performs data-access operations that do not use this data manager.

metadata.cwe_name: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component

title: CWE-1058: Invokable Control Element in Multi-Thread Context with non-Final Static Storable or Member Element
text:
CWE-1058 Invokable Control Element in Multi-Thread Context with non-Final Static Storable or Member Element
The code contains a function or method that
 operates in a multi-threaded environment but owns an unsafe non-final
 static storable or member data element.

metadata.cwe_name: Invokable Control Element in Multi-Thread Context with non-Final Static Storable or Member Element

title: CWE-1059: Insufficient Technical Documentation
text:
CWE-1059 Insufficient Technical Documentation
The product does not contain sufficient
 technical or engineering documentation (whether on paper or
 in electronic form) that contains descriptions of all the
 relevant software/hardware elements of the product, such as
 its usage, structure, architectural components, interfaces, design, implementation,
 configuration, operation, etc.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Technical Documentation

title: CWE-106: Struts: Plug-in Framework not in Use
text:
CWE-106 Struts: Plug-in Framework not in Use
When an application does not use an input validation framework such as the Struts Validator, there is a greater risk of introducing weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Plug-in Framework not in Use

title: CWE-1060: Excessive Number of Inefficient Server-Side Data Accesses
text:
CWE-1060 Excessive Number of Inefficient Server-Side Data Accesses
The product performs too many data queries without using efficient data processing functionality such as stored procedures.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Number of Inefficient Server-Side Data Accesses

title: CWE-1061: Insufficient Encapsulation
text:
CWE-1061 Insufficient Encapsulation
The product does not sufficiently hide the internal representation and implementation details of data or methods, which might allow external components or modules to modify data unexpectedly, invoke unexpected functionality, or introduce dependencies that the programmer did not intend.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Encapsulation

title: CWE-1062: Parent Class with References to Child Class
text:
CWE-1062 Parent Class with References to Child Class
The code has a parent class that contains references to a child class, its methods, or its members.

metadata.cwe_name: Parent Class with References to Child Class

title: CWE-1063: Creation of Class Instance within a Static Code Block
text:
CWE-1063 Creation of Class Instance within a Static Code Block
A static code block creates an instance of a class.

metadata.cwe_name: Creation of Class Instance within a Static Code Block

title: CWE-1064: Invokable Control Element with Signature Containing an Excessive Number of Parameters
text:
CWE-1064 Invokable Control Element with Signature Containing an Excessive Number of Parameters
The product contains a function, subroutine, or method whose signature has an unnecessarily large number of
 parameters/arguments.

metadata.cwe_name: Invokable Control Element with Signature Containing an Excessive Number of Parameters

title: CWE-1065: Runtime Resource Management Control Element in a Component Built to Run on Application Servers
text:
CWE-1065 Runtime Resource Management Control Element in a Component Built to Run on Application Servers
The product uses deployed components from application servers, but it also uses low-level functions/methods for management of resources, instead of the API provided by the application server.

metadata.cwe_name: Runtime Resource Management Control Element in a Component Built to Run on Application Servers

title: CWE-1066: Missing Serialization Control Element
text:
CWE-1066 Missing Serialization Control Element
The product contains a serializable data element that does not
 have an associated serialization method.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Serialization Control Element

title: CWE-1067: Excessive Execution of Sequential Searches of Data Resource
text:
CWE-1067 Excessive Execution of Sequential Searches of Data Resource
The product contains a data query against an SQL table or view
 that is configured in a way that does not utilize an index and may cause
 sequential searches to be performed.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Execution of Sequential Searches of Data Resource

title: CWE-1068: Inconsistency Between Implementation and Documented Design
text:
CWE-1068 Inconsistency Between Implementation and Documented Design
The implementation of the product is not consistent with the
 design as described within the relevant documentation.

metadata.cwe_name: Inconsistency Between Implementation and Documented Design

title: CWE-1069: Empty Exception Block
text:
CWE-1069 Empty Exception Block
An invokable code block contains an exception handling block that does not contain any code, i.e. is empty.

metadata.cwe_name: Empty Exception Block

title: CWE-107: Struts: Unused Validation Form
text:
CWE-107 Struts: Unused Validation Form
An unused validation form indicates that validation logic is not up-to-date.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Unused Validation Form

title: CWE-1070: Serializable Data Element Containing non-Serializable Item Elements
text:
CWE-1070 Serializable Data Element Containing non-Serializable Item Elements
The product contains a serializable, storable data element such as a field or member,
 but the data element contains member elements that are not
 serializable.

metadata.cwe_name: Serializable Data Element Containing non-Serializable Item Elements

title: CWE-1071: Empty Code Block
text:
CWE-1071 Empty Code Block
The source code contains a block that does not contain any code, i.e., the block is empty.

metadata.cwe_name: Empty Code Block

title: CWE-1072: Data Resource Access without Use of Connection Pooling
text:
CWE-1072 Data Resource Access without Use of Connection Pooling
The product accesses a data resource through a database without using a
 connection pooling capability.

metadata.cwe_name: Data Resource Access without Use of Connection Pooling

title: CWE-1073: Non-SQL Invokable Control Element with Excessive Number of Data Resource Accesses
text:
CWE-1073 Non-SQL Invokable Control Element with Excessive Number of Data Resource Accesses
The product contains a client with a function or method that contains a large number of data accesses/queries that are sent through a data manager, i.e., does not use efficient database capabilities.

metadata.cwe_name: Non-SQL Invokable Control Element with Excessive Number of Data Resource Accesses

title: CWE-1074: Class with Excessively Deep Inheritance
text:
CWE-1074 Class with Excessively Deep Inheritance
A class has an inheritance level that is too high, i.e., it
 has a large number of parent classes.

metadata.cwe_name: Class with Excessively Deep Inheritance

title: CWE-1075: Unconditional Control Flow Transfer outside of Switch Block
text:
CWE-1075 Unconditional Control Flow Transfer outside of Switch Block
The product performs unconditional control transfer (such as a
 "goto") in code outside of a branching structure such as a switch
 block.

metadata.cwe_name: Unconditional Control Flow Transfer outside of Switch Block

title: CWE-1076: Insufficient Adherence to Expected Conventions
text:
CWE-1076 Insufficient Adherence to Expected Conventions
The product's architecture, source code, design, documentation,
 or other artifact does not follow required conventions.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Adherence to Expected Conventions

title: CWE-1077: Floating Point Comparison with Incorrect Operator
text:
CWE-1077 Floating Point Comparison with Incorrect Operator
The code performs a comparison such as an
 equality test between two float (floating point) values, but
 it uses comparison operators that do not account for the
 possibility of loss of precision.

metadata.cwe_name: Floating Point Comparison with Incorrect Operator

title: CWE-1078: Inappropriate Source Code Style or Formatting
text:
CWE-1078 Inappropriate Source Code Style or Formatting
The source code does not follow
 desired style or formatting for indentation, white
 space, comments, etc.

metadata.cwe_name: Inappropriate Source Code Style or Formatting

title: CWE-1079: Parent Class without Virtual Destructor Method
text:
CWE-1079 Parent Class without Virtual Destructor Method
A parent class contains one or more child classes, but the parent class does not have a virtual destructor method.

metadata.cwe_name: Parent Class without Virtual Destructor Method

title: CWE-108: Struts: Unvalidated Action Form
text:
CWE-108 Struts: Unvalidated Action Form
Every Action Form must have a corresponding validation form.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Unvalidated Action Form

title: CWE-1080: Source Code File with Excessive Number of Lines of Code
text:
CWE-1080 Source Code File with Excessive Number of Lines of Code
A source code file has too many lines of
 code.

metadata.cwe_name: Source Code File with Excessive Number of Lines of Code

title: CWE-1082: Class Instance Self Destruction Control Element
text:
CWE-1082 Class Instance Self Destruction Control Element
The code contains a class instance that calls the method or function to delete or destroy itself.

metadata.cwe_name: Class Instance Self Destruction Control Element

title: CWE-1083: Data Access from Outside Expected Data Manager Component
text:
CWE-1083 Data Access from Outside Expected Data Manager Component
The product is intended to manage data access through a particular data manager component such as a relational or non-SQL database, but it contains code that performs data access operations without using that component.

metadata.cwe_name: Data Access from Outside Expected Data Manager Component

title: CWE-1084: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations
text:
CWE-1084 Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations
A function or method contains too many
 operations that utilize a data manager or file resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive File or Data Access Operations

title: CWE-1085: Invokable Control Element with Excessive Volume of Commented-out Code
text:
CWE-1085 Invokable Control Element with Excessive Volume of Commented-out Code
A function, method, procedure, etc. contains an excessive amount of code that has been
 commented out within its body.

metadata.cwe_name: Invokable Control Element with Excessive Volume of Commented-out Code

title: CWE-1086: Class with Excessive Number of Child Classes
text:
CWE-1086 Class with Excessive Number of Child Classes
A class contains an unnecessarily large number of
 children.

metadata.cwe_name: Class with Excessive Number of Child Classes

title: CWE-1087: Class with Virtual Method without a Virtual Destructor
text:
CWE-1087 Class with Virtual Method without a Virtual Destructor
A class contains a virtual method, but the method does not have an associated virtual destructor.

metadata.cwe_name: Class with Virtual Method without a Virtual Destructor

title: CWE-1088: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout
text:
CWE-1088 Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout
The code has a synchronous call to a remote resource, but there is no timeout for the call, or the timeout is set to infinite.

metadata.cwe_name: Synchronous Access of Remote Resource without Timeout

title: CWE-1089: Large Data Table with Excessive Number of Indices
text:
CWE-1089 Large Data Table with Excessive Number of Indices
The product uses a large data table that contains an excessively large number of
 indices.

metadata.cwe_name: Large Data Table with Excessive Number of Indices

title: CWE-109: Struts: Validator Turned Off
text:
CWE-109 Struts: Validator Turned Off
Automatic filtering via a Struts bean has been turned off, which disables the Struts Validator and custom validation logic. This exposes the application to other weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Validator Turned Off

title: CWE-1090: Method Containing Access of a Member Element from Another Class
text:
CWE-1090 Method Containing Access of a Member Element from Another Class
A method for a class performs an operation that directly
 accesses a member element from another class.

metadata.cwe_name: Method Containing Access of a Member Element from Another Class

title: CWE-1091: Use of Object without Invoking Destructor Method
text:
CWE-1091 Use of Object without Invoking Destructor Method
The product contains a method that accesses an object but does not later invoke
 the element's associated finalize/destructor method.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Object without Invoking Destructor Method

title: CWE-1092: Use of Same Invokable Control Element in Multiple Architectural Layers
text:
CWE-1092 Use of Same Invokable Control Element in Multiple Architectural Layers
The product uses the same control element across multiple
 architectural layers.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Same Invokable Control Element in Multiple Architectural Layers

title: CWE-1093: Excessively Complex Data Representation
text:
CWE-1093 Excessively Complex Data Representation
The product uses an unnecessarily complex internal representation for its data structures or interrelationships between those structures.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessively Complex Data Representation

title: CWE-1094: Excessive Index Range Scan for a Data Resource
text:
CWE-1094 Excessive Index Range Scan for a Data Resource
The product contains an index range scan for a large data table,
 but the scan can cover a large number of rows.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Index Range Scan for a Data Resource

title: CWE-1095: Loop Condition Value Update within the Loop
text:
CWE-1095 Loop Condition Value Update within the Loop
The product uses a loop with a control flow condition based on
 a value that is updated within the body of the loop.

metadata.cwe_name: Loop Condition Value Update within the Loop

title: CWE-1096: Singleton Class Instance Creation without Proper Locking or Synchronization
text:
CWE-1096 Singleton Class Instance Creation without Proper Locking or Synchronization
The product implements a Singleton design pattern but does not use appropriate locking or other synchronization mechanism to ensure that the singleton class is only instantiated once.

metadata.cwe_name: Singleton Class Instance Creation without Proper Locking or Synchronization

title: CWE-1097: Persistent Storable Data Element without Associated Comparison Control Element
text:
CWE-1097 Persistent Storable Data Element without Associated Comparison Control Element
The product uses a storable data element that does not have
 all of the associated functions or methods that are necessary to support
 comparison.

metadata.cwe_name: Persistent Storable Data Element without Associated Comparison Control Element

title: CWE-1098: Data Element containing Pointer Item without Proper Copy Control Element
text:
CWE-1098 Data Element containing Pointer Item without Proper Copy Control Element
The code contains a data element with a pointer that does not have an associated copy or constructor method.

metadata.cwe_name: Data Element containing Pointer Item without Proper Copy Control Element

title: CWE-1099: Inconsistent Naming Conventions for Identifiers
text:
CWE-1099 Inconsistent Naming Conventions for Identifiers
The product's code, documentation, or other artifacts do not
 consistently use the same naming conventions for variables, callables, groups of
 related callables, I/O capabilities, data types, file names, or similar types of
 elements.

metadata.cwe_name: Inconsistent Naming Conventions for Identifiers

title: CWE-11: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Creating Debug Binary
text:
CWE-11 ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Creating Debug Binary
Debugging messages help attackers learn about the system and plan a form of attack.

metadata.cwe_name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Creating Debug Binary

title: CWE-110: Struts: Validator Without Form Field
text:
CWE-110 Struts: Validator Without Form Field
Validation fields that do not appear in forms they are associated with indicate that the validation logic is out of date.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Validator Without Form Field

title: CWE-1100: Insufficient Isolation of System-Dependent Functions
text:
CWE-1100 Insufficient Isolation of System-Dependent Functions
The product or code does not isolate system-dependent
 functionality into separate standalone modules.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Isolation of System-Dependent Functions

title: CWE-1101: Reliance on Runtime Component in Generated Code
text:
CWE-1101 Reliance on Runtime Component in Generated Code
The product uses automatically-generated code that cannot be
 executed without a specific runtime support component.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Runtime Component in Generated Code

title: CWE-1102: Reliance on Machine-Dependent Data Representation
text:
CWE-1102 Reliance on Machine-Dependent Data Representation
The code uses a data representation that relies on low-level
 data representation or constructs that may vary across different processors,
 physical machines, OSes, or other physical components.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Machine-Dependent Data Representation

title: CWE-1103: Use of Platform-Dependent Third Party Components
text:
CWE-1103 Use of Platform-Dependent Third Party Components
The product relies on third-party components that do
 not provide equivalent functionality across all desirable
 platforms.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Platform-Dependent Third Party Components

title: CWE-1104: Use of Unmaintained Third Party Components
text:
CWE-1104 Use of Unmaintained Third Party Components
The product relies on third-party components that are not
 actively supported or maintained by the original developer or a trusted proxy
 for the original developer.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Unmaintained Third Party Components

title: CWE-1105: Insufficient Encapsulation of Machine-Dependent Functionality
text:
CWE-1105 Insufficient Encapsulation of Machine-Dependent Functionality
The product or code uses machine-dependent functionality, but
 it does not sufficiently encapsulate or isolate this functionality from
 the rest of the code.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Encapsulation of Machine-Dependent Functionality

title: CWE-1106: Insufficient Use of Symbolic Constants
text:
CWE-1106 Insufficient Use of Symbolic Constants
The source code uses literal constants that may need to change
 or evolve over time, instead of using symbolic constants.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Use of Symbolic Constants

title: CWE-1107: Insufficient Isolation of Symbolic Constant Definitions
text:
CWE-1107 Insufficient Isolation of Symbolic Constant Definitions
The source code uses symbolic constants, but it does not
 sufficiently place the definitions of these constants into a more centralized or
 isolated location.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Isolation of Symbolic Constant Definitions

title: CWE-1108: Excessive Reliance on Global Variables
text:
CWE-1108 Excessive Reliance on Global Variables
The code is structured in a way that relies too much on using
 or setting global variables throughout various points in the code, instead of
 preserving the associated information in a narrower, more local
 context.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Reliance on Global Variables

title: CWE-1109: Use of Same Variable for Multiple Purposes
text:
CWE-1109 Use of Same Variable for Multiple Purposes
The code contains a callable, block, or other code element in
 which the same variable is used to control more than one unique task or store
 more than one instance of data.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Same Variable for Multiple Purposes

title: CWE-111: Direct Use of Unsafe JNI
text:
CWE-111 Direct Use of Unsafe JNI
When a Java application uses the Java Native Interface (JNI) to call code written in another programming language, it can expose the application to weaknesses in that code, even if those weaknesses cannot occur in Java.

metadata.cwe_name: Direct Use of Unsafe JNI

title: CWE-1110: Incomplete Design Documentation
text:
CWE-1110 Incomplete Design Documentation
The product's design documentation does not adequately describe
 control flow, data flow, system initialization, relationships between tasks,
 components, rationales, or other important aspects of the
 design.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Design Documentation

title: CWE-1111: Incomplete I/O Documentation
text:
CWE-1111 Incomplete I/O Documentation
The product's documentation does not adequately define inputs,
 outputs, or system/software interfaces.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete I/O Documentation

title: CWE-1112: Incomplete Documentation of Program Execution
text:
CWE-1112 Incomplete Documentation of Program Execution
The document does not fully define all mechanisms that are used
 to control or influence how product-specific programs are
 executed.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Documentation of Program Execution

title: CWE-1113: Inappropriate Comment Style
text:
CWE-1113 Inappropriate Comment Style
The source code uses comment styles or formats that are
 inconsistent or do not follow expected standards for the
 product.

metadata.cwe_name: Inappropriate Comment Style

title: CWE-1114: Inappropriate Whitespace Style
text:
CWE-1114 Inappropriate Whitespace Style
The source code contains whitespace that is inconsistent across
 the code or does not follow expected standards for the
 product.

metadata.cwe_name: Inappropriate Whitespace Style

title: CWE-1115: Source Code Element without Standard Prologue
text:
CWE-1115 Source Code Element without Standard Prologue
The source code contains elements such as source files 
 that do not consistently provide a prologue or header that has been
 standardized for the project.

metadata.cwe_name: Source Code Element without Standard Prologue

title: CWE-1116: Inaccurate Source Code Comments
text:
CWE-1116 Inaccurate Source Code Comments
The source code contains comments that do not accurately
 describe or explain aspects of the portion of the code with which the comment is
 associated.

metadata.cwe_name: Inaccurate Source Code Comments

title: CWE-1117: Callable with Insufficient Behavioral Summary
text:
CWE-1117 Callable with Insufficient Behavioral Summary
The code contains a function or method whose signature and/or associated
 inline documentation does not sufficiently describe the callable's inputs, outputs,
 side effects, assumptions, or return codes.

metadata.cwe_name: Callable with Insufficient Behavioral Summary

title: CWE-1118: Insufficient Documentation of Error Handling Techniques
text:
CWE-1118 Insufficient Documentation of Error Handling Techniques
The documentation does not sufficiently describe the techniques
 that are used for error handling, exception processing, or similar
 mechanisms.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Documentation of Error Handling Techniques

title: CWE-1119: Excessive Use of Unconditional Branching
text:
CWE-1119 Excessive Use of Unconditional Branching
The code uses too many unconditional branches (such as
 "goto").

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Use of Unconditional Branching

title: CWE-112: Missing XML Validation
text:
CWE-112 Missing XML Validation
The product accepts XML from an untrusted source but does not validate the XML against the proper schema.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing XML Validation

title: CWE-1120: Excessive Code Complexity
text:
CWE-1120 Excessive Code Complexity
The code is too complex, as calculated using a well-defined,
 quantitative measure.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Code Complexity

title: CWE-1121: Excessive McCabe Cyclomatic Complexity
text:
CWE-1121 Excessive McCabe Cyclomatic Complexity
The code contains McCabe cyclomatic complexity that exceeds a
 desirable maximum.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive McCabe Cyclomatic Complexity

title: CWE-1122: Excessive Halstead Complexity
text:
CWE-1122 Excessive Halstead Complexity
The code is structured in a way that a Halstead complexity
 measure exceeds a desirable maximum.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Halstead Complexity

title: CWE-1123: Excessive Use of Self-Modifying Code
text:
CWE-1123 Excessive Use of Self-Modifying Code
The product uses too much self-modifying code.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Use of Self-Modifying Code

title: CWE-1124: Excessively Deep Nesting
text:
CWE-1124 Excessively Deep Nesting
The code contains a callable or other code grouping in which
 the nesting / branching is too deep.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessively Deep Nesting

title: CWE-1125: Excessive Attack Surface
text:
CWE-1125 Excessive Attack Surface
The product has an attack surface whose quantitative
 measurement exceeds a desirable maximum.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Attack Surface

title: CWE-1126: Declaration of Variable with Unnecessarily Wide Scope
text:
CWE-1126 Declaration of Variable with Unnecessarily Wide Scope
The source code declares a variable in one scope, but the
 variable is only used within a narrower scope.

metadata.cwe_name: Declaration of Variable with Unnecessarily Wide Scope

title: CWE-1127: Compilation with Insufficient Warnings or Errors
text:
CWE-1127 Compilation with Insufficient Warnings or Errors
The code is compiled without sufficient warnings enabled, which
 may prevent the detection of subtle bugs or quality
 issues.

metadata.cwe_name: Compilation with Insufficient Warnings or Errors

title: CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Request/Response Splitting')
text:
CWE-113 Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Request/Response Splitting')
The product receives data from an HTTP agent/component (e.g., web server, proxy, browser, etc.), but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CR and LF characters before the data is included in outgoing HTTP headers.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Request/Response Splitting')

title: CWE-114: Process Control
text:
CWE-114 Process Control
Executing commands or loading libraries from an untrusted source or in an untrusted environment can cause an application to execute malicious commands (and payloads) on behalf of an attacker.

metadata.cwe_name: Process Control

title: CWE-115: Misinterpretation of Input
text:
CWE-115 Misinterpretation of Input
The product misinterprets an input, whether from an attacker or another product, in a security-relevant fashion.

metadata.cwe_name: Misinterpretation of Input

title: CWE-116: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
text:
CWE-116 Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
The product prepares a structured message for communication with another component, but encoding or escaping of the data is either missing or done incorrectly. As a result, the intended structure of the message is not preserved.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output

title: CWE-1164: Irrelevant Code
text:
CWE-1164 Irrelevant Code
The product contains code that is not essential for execution,
 i.e. makes no state changes and has no side effects that alter
 data or control flow, such that removal of the code would have no impact
 to functionality or correctness.

metadata.cwe_name: Irrelevant Code

title: CWE-117: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
text:
CWE-117 Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
The product constructs a log message from external input, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements when the message is written to a log file.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs

title: CWE-1173: Improper Use of Validation Framework
text:
CWE-1173 Improper Use of Validation Framework
The product does not use, or incorrectly uses, an input validation framework that is provided by the source language or an independent library.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Use of Validation Framework

title: CWE-1174: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Improper Model Validation
text:
CWE-1174 ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Improper Model Validation
The ASP.NET application does not use, or incorrectly uses, the model validation framework.

metadata.cwe_name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Improper Model Validation

title: CWE-1176: Inefficient CPU Computation
text:
CWE-1176 Inefficient CPU Computation
The product performs CPU computations using
 algorithms that are not as efficient as they could be for the
 needs of the developer, i.e., the computations can be
 optimized further.

metadata.cwe_name: Inefficient CPU Computation

title: CWE-1177: Use of Prohibited Code
text:
CWE-1177 Use of Prohibited Code
The product uses a function, library, or third party component
 that has been explicitly prohibited, whether by the developer or
 the customer.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Prohibited Code

title: CWE-118: Incorrect Access of Indexable Resource ('Range Error')
text:
CWE-118 Incorrect Access of Indexable Resource ('Range Error')
The product does not restrict or incorrectly restricts operations within the boundaries of a resource that is accessed using an index or pointer, such as memory or files.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Access of Indexable Resource ('Range Error')

title: CWE-1187: DEPRECATED: Use of Uninitialized Resource
text:
CWE-1187 DEPRECATED: Use of Uninitialized Resource
This entry has been deprecated because it was a duplicate of CWE-908. All content has been transferred to CWE-908.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Use of Uninitialized Resource

title: CWE-1188: Initialization of a Resource with an Insecure Default
text:
CWE-1188 Initialization of a Resource with an Insecure Default
The product initializes or sets a resource with a default that is intended to be changed by the product's installer, administrator, or maintainer, but the default is not secure.

metadata.cwe_name: Initialization of a Resource with an Insecure Default

title: CWE-1189: Improper Isolation of Shared Resources on System-on-a-Chip (SoC)
text:
CWE-1189 Improper Isolation of Shared Resources on System-on-a-Chip (SoC)
The System-On-a-Chip (SoC) does not properly isolate shared resources between trusted and untrusted agents.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Isolation of Shared Resources on System-on-a-Chip (SoC)

title: CWE-119: Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
text:
CWE-119 Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
The product performs operations on a memory buffer, but it reads from or writes to a memory location outside the buffer's intended boundary. This may result in read or write operations on unexpected memory locations that could be linked to other variables, data structures, or internal program data.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer

title: CWE-1190: DMA Device Enabled Too Early in Boot Phase
text:
CWE-1190 DMA Device Enabled Too Early in Boot Phase
The product enables a Direct Memory Access (DMA) capable device before the security configuration settings are established, which allows an attacker to extract data from or gain privileges on the product.

metadata.cwe_name: DMA Device Enabled Too Early in Boot Phase

title: CWE-1191: On-Chip Debug and Test Interface With Improper Access Control
text:
CWE-1191 On-Chip Debug and Test Interface With Improper Access Control
The chip does not implement or does not correctly perform access control to check whether users are authorized to access internal registers and test modes through the physical debug/test interface.

metadata.cwe_name: On-Chip Debug and Test Interface With Improper Access Control

title: CWE-1192: Improper Identifier for IP Block used in System-On-Chip (SOC)
text:
CWE-1192 Improper Identifier for IP Block used in System-On-Chip (SOC)
The System-on-Chip (SoC) does not have unique, immutable identifiers for each of its components.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Identifier for IP Block used in System-On-Chip (SOC)

title: CWE-1193: Power-On of Untrusted Execution Core Before Enabling Fabric Access Control
text:
CWE-1193 Power-On of Untrusted Execution Core Before Enabling Fabric Access Control
The product enables components that contain untrusted firmware before memory and fabric access controls have been enabled.

metadata.cwe_name: Power-On of Untrusted Execution Core Before Enabling Fabric Access Control

title: CWE-12: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Missing Custom Error Page
text:
CWE-12 ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Missing Custom Error Page
An ASP .NET application must enable custom error pages in order to prevent attackers from mining information from the framework's built-in responses.

metadata.cwe_name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Missing Custom Error Page

title: CWE-120: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
text:
CWE-120 Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
The product copies an input buffer to an output buffer without verifying that the size of the input buffer is less than the size of the output buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')

title: CWE-1204: Generation of Weak Initialization Vector (IV)
text:
CWE-1204 Generation of Weak Initialization Vector (IV)
The product uses a cryptographic primitive that uses an Initialization
 Vector (IV), but the product does not generate IVs that are
 sufficiently unpredictable or unique according to the expected
 cryptographic requirements for that primitive.

metadata.cwe_name: Generation of Weak Initialization Vector (IV)

title: CWE-1209: Failure to Disable Reserved Bits
text:
CWE-1209 Failure to Disable Reserved Bits
The reserved bits in a hardware design are not disabled prior to production. Typically, reserved bits are used for future capabilities and should not support any functional logic in the design. However, designers might covertly use these bits to debug or further develop new capabilities in production hardware. Adversaries with access to these bits will write to them in hopes of compromising hardware state.

metadata.cwe_name: Failure to Disable Reserved Bits

title: CWE-121: Stack-based Buffer Overflow
text:
CWE-121 Stack-based Buffer Overflow
A stack-based buffer overflow condition is a condition where the buffer being overwritten is allocated on the stack (i.e., is a local variable or, rarely, a parameter to a function).

metadata.cwe_name: Stack-based Buffer Overflow

title: CWE-122: Heap-based Buffer Overflow
text:
CWE-122 Heap-based Buffer Overflow
A heap overflow condition is a buffer overflow, where the buffer that can be overwritten is allocated in the heap portion of memory, generally meaning that the buffer was allocated using a routine such as malloc().

metadata.cwe_name: Heap-based Buffer Overflow

title: CWE-1220: Insufficient Granularity of Access Control
text:
CWE-1220 Insufficient Granularity of Access Control
The product implements access controls via a policy or other feature with the intention to disable or restrict accesses (reads and/or writes) to assets in a system from untrusted agents. However, implemented access controls lack required granularity, which renders the control policy too broad because it allows accesses from unauthorized agents to the security-sensitive assets.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Granularity of Access Control

title: CWE-1221: Incorrect Register Defaults or Module Parameters
text:
CWE-1221 Incorrect Register Defaults or Module Parameters
Hardware description language code incorrectly defines register defaults or hardware Intellectual Property (IP) parameters to insecure values.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Register Defaults or Module Parameters

title: CWE-1222: Insufficient Granularity of Address Regions Protected by Register Locks
text:
CWE-1222 Insufficient Granularity of Address Regions Protected by Register Locks
The product defines a large address region protected from modification by the same register lock control bit. This results in a conflict between the functional requirement that some addresses need to be writable by software during operation and the security requirement that the system configuration lock bit must be set during the boot process.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Granularity of Address Regions Protected by Register Locks

title: CWE-1223: Race Condition for Write-Once Attributes
text:
CWE-1223 Race Condition for Write-Once Attributes
A write-once register in hardware design is programmable by an untrusted software component earlier than the trusted software component, resulting in a race condition issue.

metadata.cwe_name: Race Condition for Write-Once Attributes

title: CWE-1224: Improper Restriction of Write-Once Bit Fields
text:
CWE-1224 Improper Restriction of Write-Once Bit Fields
The hardware design control register "sticky bits" or write-once bit fields are improperly implemented, such that they can be reprogrammed by software.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Write-Once Bit Fields

title: CWE-1229: Creation of Emergent Resource
text:
CWE-1229 Creation of Emergent Resource
The product manages resources or behaves in a way that indirectly creates a new, distinct resource that can be used by attackers in violation of the intended policy.

metadata.cwe_name: Creation of Emergent Resource

title: CWE-123: Write-what-where Condition
text:
CWE-123 Write-what-where Condition
Any condition where the attacker has the ability to write an arbitrary value to an arbitrary location, often as the result of a buffer overflow.

metadata.cwe_name: Write-what-where Condition

title: CWE-1230: Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Metadata
text:
CWE-1230 Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Metadata
The product prevents direct access to a resource containing sensitive information, but it does not sufficiently limit access to metadata that is derived from the original, sensitive information.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Metadata

title: CWE-1231: Improper Prevention of Lock Bit Modification
text:
CWE-1231 Improper Prevention of Lock Bit Modification
The product uses a trusted lock bit for restricting access to registers, address regions, or other resources, but the product does not prevent the value of the lock bit from being modified after it has been set.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Prevention of Lock Bit Modification

title: CWE-1232: Improper Lock Behavior After Power State Transition
text:
CWE-1232 Improper Lock Behavior After Power State Transition
Register lock bit protection disables changes to system configuration once the bit is set. Some of the protected registers or lock bits become programmable after power state transitions (e.g., Entry and wake from low power sleep modes) causing the system configuration to be changeable.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Lock Behavior After Power State Transition

title: CWE-1233: Security-Sensitive Hardware Controls with Missing Lock Bit Protection
text:
CWE-1233 Security-Sensitive Hardware Controls with Missing Lock Bit Protection
The product uses a register lock bit protection mechanism, but it does not ensure that the lock bit prevents modification of system registers or controls that perform changes to important hardware system configuration.

metadata.cwe_name: Security-Sensitive Hardware Controls with Missing Lock Bit Protection

title: CWE-1234: Hardware Internal or Debug Modes Allow Override of Locks
text:
CWE-1234 Hardware Internal or Debug Modes Allow Override of Locks
System configuration protection may be bypassed during debug mode.

metadata.cwe_name: Hardware Internal or Debug Modes Allow Override of Locks

title: CWE-1235: Incorrect Use of Autoboxing and Unboxing for Performance Critical Operations
text:
CWE-1235 Incorrect Use of Autoboxing and Unboxing for Performance Critical Operations
The code uses boxed primitives, which may introduce inefficiencies into performance-critical operations.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Use of Autoboxing and Unboxing for Performance Critical Operations

title: CWE-1236: Improper Neutralization of Formula Elements in a CSV File
text:
CWE-1236 Improper Neutralization of Formula Elements in a CSV File
The product saves user-provided information into a Comma-Separated Value (CSV) file, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as a command when the file is opened by a spreadsheet product.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Formula Elements in a CSV File

title: CWE-1239: Improper Zeroization of Hardware Register
text:
CWE-1239 Improper Zeroization of Hardware Register
The hardware product does not properly clear sensitive information from built-in registers when the user of the hardware block changes.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Zeroization of Hardware Register

title: CWE-124: Buffer Underwrite ('Buffer Underflow')
text:
CWE-124 Buffer Underwrite ('Buffer Underflow')
The product writes to a buffer using an index or pointer that references a memory location prior to the beginning of the buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Buffer Underwrite ('Buffer Underflow')

title: CWE-1240: Use of a Cryptographic Primitive with a Risky Implementation
text:
CWE-1240 Use of a Cryptographic Primitive with a Risky Implementation
To fulfill the need for a cryptographic primitive, the product implements a cryptographic algorithm using a non-standard, unproven, or disallowed/non-compliant cryptographic implementation.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of a Cryptographic Primitive with a Risky Implementation

title: CWE-1241: Use of Predictable Algorithm in Random Number Generator
text:
CWE-1241 Use of Predictable Algorithm in Random Number Generator
The device uses an algorithm that is predictable and generates a pseudo-random number.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Predictable Algorithm in Random Number Generator

title: CWE-1242: Inclusion of Undocumented Features or Chicken Bits
text:
CWE-1242 Inclusion of Undocumented Features or Chicken Bits
The device includes chicken bits or undocumented features that can create entry points for unauthorized actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Inclusion of Undocumented Features or Chicken Bits

title: CWE-1243: Sensitive Non-Volatile Information Not Protected During Debug
text:
CWE-1243 Sensitive Non-Volatile Information Not Protected During Debug
Access to security-sensitive information stored in fuses is not limited during debug.

metadata.cwe_name: Sensitive Non-Volatile Information Not Protected During Debug

title: CWE-1244: Internal Asset Exposed to Unsafe Debug Access Level or State
text:
CWE-1244 Internal Asset Exposed to Unsafe Debug Access Level or State
The product uses physical debug or test
 interfaces with support for multiple access levels, but it
 assigns the wrong debug access level to an internal asset,
 providing unintended access to the asset from untrusted debug
 agents.

metadata.cwe_name: Internal Asset Exposed to Unsafe Debug Access Level or State

title: CWE-1245: Improper Finite State Machines (FSMs) in Hardware Logic
text:
CWE-1245 Improper Finite State Machines (FSMs) in Hardware Logic
Faulty finite state machines (FSMs) in the hardware logic allow an attacker to put the system in an undefined state, to cause a denial of service (DoS) or gain privileges on the victim's system.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Finite State Machines (FSMs) in Hardware Logic

title: CWE-1246: Improper Write Handling in Limited-write Non-Volatile Memories
text:
CWE-1246 Improper Write Handling in Limited-write Non-Volatile Memories
The product does not implement or incorrectly implements wear leveling operations in limited-write non-volatile memories.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Write Handling in Limited-write Non-Volatile Memories

title: CWE-1247: Improper Protection Against Voltage and Clock Glitches
text:
CWE-1247 Improper Protection Against Voltage and Clock Glitches
The device does not contain or contains incorrectly implemented circuitry or sensors to detect and mitigate voltage and clock glitches and protect sensitive information or software contained on the device.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Protection Against Voltage and Clock Glitches

title: CWE-1248: Semiconductor Defects in Hardware Logic with Security-Sensitive Implications
text:
CWE-1248 Semiconductor Defects in Hardware Logic with Security-Sensitive Implications
The security-sensitive hardware module contains semiconductor defects.

metadata.cwe_name: Semiconductor Defects in Hardware Logic with Security-Sensitive Implications

title: CWE-1249: Application-Level Admin Tool with Inconsistent View of Underlying Operating System
text:
CWE-1249 Application-Level Admin Tool with Inconsistent View of Underlying Operating System
The product provides an application for administrators to manage parts of the underlying operating system, but the application does not accurately identify all of the relevant entities or resources that exist in the OS; that is, the application's model of the OS's state is inconsistent with the OS's actual state.

metadata.cwe_name: Application-Level Admin Tool with Inconsistent View of Underlying Operating System

title: CWE-125: Out-of-bounds Read
text:
CWE-125 Out-of-bounds Read
The product reads data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Out-of-bounds Read

title: CWE-1250: Improper Preservation of Consistency Between Independent Representations of Shared State
text:
CWE-1250 Improper Preservation of Consistency Between Independent Representations of Shared State
The product has or supports multiple distributed components or sub-systems that are each required to keep their own local copy of shared data - such as state or cache - but the product does not ensure that all local copies remain consistent with each other.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Preservation of Consistency Between Independent Representations of Shared State

title: CWE-1251: Mirrored Regions with Different Values
text:
CWE-1251 Mirrored Regions with Different Values
The product's architecture mirrors regions without ensuring that their contents always stay in sync.

metadata.cwe_name: Mirrored Regions with Different Values

title: CWE-1252: CPU Hardware Not Configured to Support Exclusivity of Write and Execute Operations
text:
CWE-1252 CPU Hardware Not Configured to Support Exclusivity of Write and Execute Operations
The CPU is not configured to provide hardware support for exclusivity of write and execute operations on memory. This allows an attacker to execute data from all of memory.

metadata.cwe_name: CPU Hardware Not Configured to Support Exclusivity of Write and Execute Operations

title: CWE-1253: Incorrect Selection of Fuse Values
text:
CWE-1253 Incorrect Selection of Fuse Values
The logic level used to set a system to a secure state relies on a fuse being unblown.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Selection of Fuse Values

title: CWE-1254: Incorrect Comparison Logic Granularity
text:
CWE-1254 Incorrect Comparison Logic Granularity
The product's comparison logic is performed over a series of steps rather than across the entire string in one operation. If there is a comparison logic failure on one of these steps, the operation may be vulnerable to a timing attack that can result in the interception of the process for nefarious purposes.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Comparison Logic Granularity

title: CWE-1255: Comparison Logic is Vulnerable to Power Side-Channel Attacks
text:
CWE-1255 Comparison Logic is Vulnerable to Power Side-Channel Attacks
A device's real time power consumption may be monitored during security token evaluation and the information gleaned may be used to determine the value of the reference token.

metadata.cwe_name: Comparison Logic is Vulnerable to Power Side-Channel Attacks

title: CWE-1256: Improper Restriction of Software Interfaces to Hardware Features
text:
CWE-1256 Improper Restriction of Software Interfaces to Hardware Features
The product provides software-controllable
 device functionality for capabilities such as power and
 clock management, but it does not properly limit
 functionality that can lead to modification of
 hardware memory or register bits, or the ability to
 observe physical side channels.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Software Interfaces to Hardware Features

title: CWE-1257: Improper Access Control Applied to Mirrored or Aliased Memory Regions
text:
CWE-1257 Improper Access Control Applied to Mirrored or Aliased Memory Regions
Aliased or mirrored memory regions in hardware designs may have inconsistent read/write permissions enforced by the hardware. A possible result is that an untrusted agent is blocked from accessing a memory region but is not blocked from accessing the corresponding aliased memory region.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Access Control Applied to Mirrored or Aliased Memory Regions

title: CWE-1258: Exposure of Sensitive System Information Due to Uncleared Debug Information
text:
CWE-1258 Exposure of Sensitive System Information Due to Uncleared Debug Information
The hardware does not fully clear security-sensitive values, such as keys and intermediate values in cryptographic operations, when debug mode is entered.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Sensitive System Information Due to Uncleared Debug Information

title: CWE-1259: Improper Restriction of Security Token Assignment
text:
CWE-1259 Improper Restriction of Security Token Assignment
The System-On-A-Chip (SoC) implements a Security Token mechanism to differentiate what actions are allowed or disallowed when a transaction originates from an entity. However, the Security Tokens are improperly protected.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Security Token Assignment

title: CWE-126: Buffer Over-read
text:
CWE-126 Buffer Over-read
The product reads from a buffer using buffer access mechanisms such as indexes or pointers that reference memory locations after the targeted buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Buffer Over-read

title: CWE-1260: Improper Handling of Overlap Between Protected Memory Ranges
text:
CWE-1260 Improper Handling of Overlap Between Protected Memory Ranges
The product allows address regions to overlap, which can result in the bypassing of intended memory protection.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Overlap Between Protected Memory Ranges

title: CWE-1261: Improper Handling of Single Event Upsets
text:
CWE-1261 Improper Handling of Single Event Upsets
The hardware logic does not effectively handle when single-event upsets (SEUs) occur.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Single Event Upsets

title: CWE-1262: Improper Access Control for Register Interface
text:
CWE-1262 Improper Access Control for Register Interface
The product uses memory-mapped I/O registers that act as an interface to hardware functionality from software, but there is improper access control to those registers.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Access Control for Register Interface

title: CWE-1263: Improper Physical Access Control
text:
CWE-1263 Improper Physical Access Control
The product is designed with access restricted to certain information, but it does not sufficiently protect against an unauthorized actor with physical access to these areas.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Physical Access Control

title: CWE-1264: Hardware Logic with Insecure De-Synchronization between Control and Data Channels
text:
CWE-1264 Hardware Logic with Insecure De-Synchronization between Control and Data Channels
The hardware logic for error handling and security checks can incorrectly forward data before the security check is complete.

metadata.cwe_name: Hardware Logic with Insecure De-Synchronization between Control and Data Channels

title: CWE-1265: Unintended Reentrant Invocation of Non-reentrant Code Via Nested Calls
text:
CWE-1265 Unintended Reentrant Invocation of Non-reentrant Code Via Nested Calls
The product invokes code that is believed to be reentrant, but the code performs a call that unintentionally produces a nested invocation of the non-reentrant code.

metadata.cwe_name: Unintended Reentrant Invocation of Non-reentrant Code Via Nested Calls

title: CWE-1266: Improper Scrubbing of Sensitive Data from Decommissioned Device
text:
CWE-1266 Improper Scrubbing of Sensitive Data from Decommissioned Device
The product does not properly provide a capability for the product administrator to remove sensitive data at the time the product is decommissioned. A scrubbing capability could be missing, insufficient, or incorrect.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Scrubbing of Sensitive Data from Decommissioned Device

title: CWE-1267: Policy Uses Obsolete Encoding
text:
CWE-1267 Policy Uses Obsolete Encoding
The product uses an obsolete encoding mechanism to implement access controls.

metadata.cwe_name: Policy Uses Obsolete Encoding

title: CWE-1268: Policy Privileges are not Assigned Consistently Between Control and Data Agents
text:
CWE-1268 Policy Privileges are not Assigned Consistently Between Control and Data Agents
The product's hardware-enforced access control for a particular resource improperly accounts for privilege discrepancies between control and write policies.

metadata.cwe_name: Policy Privileges are not Assigned Consistently Between Control and Data Agents

title: CWE-1269: Product Released in Non-Release Configuration
text:
CWE-1269 Product Released in Non-Release Configuration
The product released to market is released in pre-production or manufacturing configuration.

metadata.cwe_name: Product Released in Non-Release Configuration

title: CWE-127: Buffer Under-read
text:
CWE-127 Buffer Under-read
The product reads from a buffer using buffer access mechanisms such as indexes or pointers that reference memory locations prior to the targeted buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Buffer Under-read

title: CWE-1270: Generation of Incorrect Security Tokens
text:
CWE-1270 Generation of Incorrect Security Tokens
The product implements a Security Token mechanism to differentiate what actions are allowed or disallowed when a transaction originates from an entity. However, the Security Tokens generated in the system are incorrect.

metadata.cwe_name: Generation of Incorrect Security Tokens

title: CWE-1271: Uninitialized Value on Reset for Registers Holding Security Settings
text:
CWE-1271 Uninitialized Value on Reset for Registers Holding Security Settings
Security-critical logic is not set to a known value on reset.

metadata.cwe_name: Uninitialized Value on Reset for Registers Holding Security Settings

title: CWE-1272: Sensitive Information Uncleared Before Debug/Power State Transition
text:
CWE-1272 Sensitive Information Uncleared Before Debug/Power State Transition
The product performs a power or debug state transition, but it does not clear sensitive information that should no longer be accessible due to changes to information access restrictions.

metadata.cwe_name: Sensitive Information Uncleared Before Debug/Power State Transition

title: CWE-1273: Device Unlock Credential Sharing
text:
CWE-1273 Device Unlock Credential Sharing
The credentials necessary for unlocking a device are shared across multiple parties and may expose sensitive information.

metadata.cwe_name: Device Unlock Credential Sharing

title: CWE-1274: Improper Access Control for Volatile Memory Containing Boot Code
text:
CWE-1274 Improper Access Control for Volatile Memory Containing Boot Code
The product conducts a secure-boot process that transfers bootloader code from Non-Volatile Memory (NVM) into Volatile Memory (VM), but it does not have sufficient access control or other protections for the Volatile Memory.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Access Control for Volatile Memory Containing Boot Code

title: CWE-1275: Sensitive Cookie with Improper SameSite Attribute
text:
CWE-1275 Sensitive Cookie with Improper SameSite Attribute
The SameSite attribute for sensitive cookies is not set, or an insecure value is used.

metadata.cwe_name: Sensitive Cookie with Improper SameSite Attribute

title: CWE-1276: Hardware Child Block Incorrectly Connected to Parent System
text:
CWE-1276 Hardware Child Block Incorrectly Connected to Parent System
Signals between a hardware IP and the parent system design are incorrectly connected causing security risks.

metadata.cwe_name: Hardware Child Block Incorrectly Connected to Parent System

title: CWE-1277: Firmware Not Updateable
text:
CWE-1277 Firmware Not Updateable
The product does not provide its
 users with the ability to update or patch its
 firmware to address any vulnerabilities or
 weaknesses that may be present.

metadata.cwe_name: Firmware Not Updateable

title: CWE-1278: Missing Protection Against Hardware Reverse Engineering Using Integrated Circuit (IC) Imaging Techniques
text:
CWE-1278 Missing Protection Against Hardware Reverse Engineering Using Integrated Circuit (IC) Imaging Techniques
Information stored in hardware may be recovered by an attacker with the capability to capture and analyze images of the integrated circuit using techniques such as scanning electron microscopy.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Protection Against Hardware Reverse Engineering Using Integrated Circuit (IC) Imaging Techniques

title: CWE-1279: Cryptographic Operations are run Before Supporting Units are Ready
text:
CWE-1279 Cryptographic Operations are run Before Supporting Units are Ready
Performing cryptographic operations without ensuring that the supporting inputs are ready to supply valid data may compromise the cryptographic result.

metadata.cwe_name: Cryptographic Operations are run Before Supporting Units are Ready

title: CWE-128: Wrap-around Error
text:
CWE-128 Wrap-around Error
Wrap around errors occur whenever a value is incremented past the maximum value for its type and therefore "wraps around" to a very small, negative, or undefined value.

metadata.cwe_name: Wrap-around Error

title: CWE-1280: Access Control Check Implemented After Asset is Accessed
text:
CWE-1280 Access Control Check Implemented After Asset is Accessed
A product's hardware-based access control check occurs after the asset has been accessed.

metadata.cwe_name: Access Control Check Implemented After Asset is Accessed

title: CWE-1281: Sequence of Processor Instructions Leads to Unexpected Behavior
text:
CWE-1281 Sequence of Processor Instructions Leads to Unexpected Behavior
Specific combinations of processor instructions lead to undesirable behavior such as locking the processor until a hard reset performed.

metadata.cwe_name: Sequence of Processor Instructions Leads to Unexpected Behavior

title: CWE-1282: Assumed-Immutable Data is Stored in Writable Memory
text:
CWE-1282 Assumed-Immutable Data is Stored in Writable Memory
Immutable data, such as a first-stage bootloader, device identifiers, and "write-once" configuration settings are stored in writable memory that can be re-programmed or updated in the field.

metadata.cwe_name: Assumed-Immutable Data is Stored in Writable Memory

title: CWE-1283: Mutable Attestation or Measurement Reporting Data
text:
CWE-1283 Mutable Attestation or Measurement Reporting Data
The register contents used for attestation or measurement reporting data to verify boot flow are modifiable by an adversary.

metadata.cwe_name: Mutable Attestation or Measurement Reporting Data

title: CWE-1284: Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input
text:
CWE-1284 Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input
The product receives input that is expected to specify a quantity (such as size or length), but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the quantity has the required properties.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Specified Quantity in Input

title: CWE-1285: Improper Validation of Specified Index, Position, or Offset in Input
text:
CWE-1285 Improper Validation of Specified Index, Position, or Offset in Input
The product receives input that is expected to specify an index, position, or offset into an indexable resource such as a buffer or file, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the specified index/position/offset has the required properties.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Specified Index, Position, or Offset in Input

title: CWE-1286: Improper Validation of Syntactic Correctness of Input
text:
CWE-1286 Improper Validation of Syntactic Correctness of Input
The product receives input that is expected to be well-formed - i.e., to comply with a certain syntax - but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input complies with the syntax.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Syntactic Correctness of Input

title: CWE-1287: Improper Validation of Specified Type of Input
text:
CWE-1287 Improper Validation of Specified Type of Input
The product receives input that is expected to be of a certain type, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input is actually of the expected type.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Specified Type of Input

title: CWE-1288: Improper Validation of Consistency within Input
text:
CWE-1288 Improper Validation of Consistency within Input
The product receives a complex input with multiple elements or fields that must be consistent with each other, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input is actually consistent.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Consistency within Input

title: CWE-1289: Improper Validation of Unsafe Equivalence in Input
text:
CWE-1289 Improper Validation of Unsafe Equivalence in Input
The product receives an input value that is used as a resource identifier or other type of reference, but it does not validate or incorrectly validates that the input is equivalent to a potentially-unsafe value.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Unsafe Equivalence in Input

title: CWE-129: Improper Validation of Array Index
text:
CWE-129 Improper Validation of Array Index
The product uses untrusted input when calculating or using an array index, but the product does not validate or incorrectly validates the index to ensure the index references a valid position within the array.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Array Index

title: CWE-1290: Incorrect Decoding of Security Identifiers
text:
CWE-1290 Incorrect Decoding of Security Identifiers
The product implements a decoding mechanism to decode certain bus-transaction signals to security identifiers. If the decoding is implemented incorrectly, then untrusted agents can now gain unauthorized access to the asset.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Decoding of Security Identifiers

title: CWE-1291: Public Key Re-Use for Signing both Debug and Production Code
text:
CWE-1291 Public Key Re-Use for Signing both Debug and Production Code
The same public key is used for signing both debug and production code.

metadata.cwe_name: Public Key Re-Use for Signing both Debug and Production Code

title: CWE-1292: Incorrect Conversion of Security Identifiers
text:
CWE-1292 Incorrect Conversion of Security Identifiers
The product implements a conversion mechanism to map certain bus-transaction signals to security identifiers. However, if the conversion is incorrectly implemented, untrusted agents can gain unauthorized access to the asset.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Conversion of Security Identifiers

title: CWE-1293: Missing Source Correlation of Multiple Independent Data
text:
CWE-1293 Missing Source Correlation of Multiple Independent Data
The product relies on one source of data, preventing the ability to detect if an adversary has compromised a data source.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Source Correlation of Multiple Independent Data

title: CWE-1294: Insecure Security Identifier Mechanism
text:
CWE-1294 Insecure Security Identifier Mechanism
The System-on-Chip (SoC) implements a Security Identifier mechanism to differentiate what actions are allowed or disallowed when a transaction originates from an entity. However, the Security Identifiers are not correctly implemented.

metadata.cwe_name: Insecure Security Identifier Mechanism

title: CWE-1295: Debug Messages Revealing Unnecessary Information
text:
CWE-1295 Debug Messages Revealing Unnecessary Information
The product fails to adequately prevent the revealing of unnecessary and potentially sensitive system information within debugging messages.

metadata.cwe_name: Debug Messages Revealing Unnecessary Information

title: CWE-1296: Incorrect Chaining or Granularity of Debug Components
text:
CWE-1296 Incorrect Chaining or Granularity of Debug Components
The product's debug components contain incorrect chaining or granularity of debug components.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Chaining or Granularity of Debug Components

title: CWE-1297: Unprotected Confidential Information on Device is Accessible by OSAT Vendors
text:
CWE-1297 Unprotected Confidential Information on Device is Accessible by OSAT Vendors
The product does not adequately protect confidential information on the device from being accessed by Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) vendors.

metadata.cwe_name: Unprotected Confidential Information on Device is Accessible by OSAT Vendors

title: CWE-1298: Hardware Logic Contains Race Conditions
text:
CWE-1298 Hardware Logic Contains Race Conditions
A race condition in the hardware logic results in undermining security guarantees of the system.

metadata.cwe_name: Hardware Logic Contains Race Conditions

title: CWE-1299: Missing Protection Mechanism for Alternate Hardware Interface
text:
CWE-1299 Missing Protection Mechanism for Alternate Hardware Interface
The lack of protections on alternate paths to access
 control-protected assets (such as unprotected shadow registers
 and other external facing unguarded interfaces) allows an
 attacker to bypass existing protections to the asset that are
 only performed against the primary path.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Protection Mechanism for Alternate Hardware Interface

title: CWE-13: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Password in Configuration File
text:
CWE-13 ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Password in Configuration File
Storing a plaintext password in a configuration file allows anyone who can read the file access to the password-protected resource making them an easy target for attackers.

metadata.cwe_name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Password in Configuration File

title: CWE-130: Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency
text:
CWE-130 Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency
The product parses a formatted message or structure, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles a length field that is inconsistent with the actual length of the associated data.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency

title: CWE-1300: Improper Protection of Physical Side Channels
text:
CWE-1300 Improper Protection of Physical Side Channels
The device does not contain sufficient protection
 mechanisms to prevent physical side channels from exposing
 sensitive information due to patterns in physically observable
 phenomena such as variations in power consumption,
 electromagnetic emissions (EME), or acoustic emissions.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Protection of Physical Side Channels

title: CWE-1301: Insufficient or Incomplete Data Removal within Hardware Component
text:
CWE-1301 Insufficient or Incomplete Data Removal within Hardware Component
The product's data removal process does not completely delete all data and potentially sensitive information within hardware components.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient or Incomplete Data Removal within Hardware Component

title: CWE-1302: Missing Source Identifier in Entity Transactions on a System-On-Chip (SOC)
text:
CWE-1302 Missing Source Identifier in Entity Transactions on a System-On-Chip (SOC)
The product implements a security identifier mechanism to differentiate what actions are allowed or disallowed when a transaction originates from an entity. A transaction is sent without a security identifier.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Source Identifier in Entity Transactions on a System-On-Chip (SOC)

title: CWE-1303: Non-Transparent Sharing of Microarchitectural Resources
text:
