title: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
text:
CWE-306 Missing Authentication for Critical Function
The product does not perform any authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity or consumes a significant amount of resources.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

title: CWE-307: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
text:
CWE-307 Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
The product does not implement sufficient measures to prevent multiple failed authentication attempts within a short time frame.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts

title: CWE-308: Use of Single-factor Authentication
text:
CWE-308 Use of Single-factor Authentication
The product uses an authentication algorithm that uses a single factor (e.g., a password) in a security context that should require more than one factor.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Single-factor Authentication

title: CWE-309: Use of Password System for Primary Authentication
text:
CWE-309 Use of Password System for Primary Authentication
The use of password systems as the primary means of authentication may be subject to several flaws or shortcomings, each reducing the effectiveness of the mechanism.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Password System for Primary Authentication

title: CWE-31: Path Traversal: 'dir\..\..\filename'
text:
CWE-31 Path Traversal: 'dir\..\..\filename'
The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize 'dir\..\..\filename' (multiple internal backslash dot dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: 'dir\..\..\filename'

title: CWE-311: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
text:
CWE-311 Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
The product does not encrypt sensitive or critical information before storage or transmission.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data

title: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-312 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
The product stores sensitive information in cleartext within a resource that might be accessible to another control sphere.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

title: CWE-313: Cleartext Storage in a File or on Disk
text:
CWE-313 Cleartext Storage in a File or on Disk
The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in a file, or on disk.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage in a File or on Disk

title: CWE-314: Cleartext Storage in the Registry
text:
CWE-314 Cleartext Storage in the Registry
The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in the registry.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage in the Registry

title: CWE-315: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in a Cookie
text:
CWE-315 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in a Cookie
The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in a cookie.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in a Cookie

title: CWE-316: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Memory
text:
CWE-316 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Memory
The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in memory.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Memory

title: CWE-317: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in GUI
text:
CWE-317 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in GUI
The product stores sensitive information in cleartext within the GUI.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in GUI

title: CWE-318: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Executable
text:
CWE-318 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Executable
The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in an executable.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Executable

title: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-319 Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
The product transmits sensitive or security-critical data in cleartext in a communication channel that can be sniffed by unauthorized actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information

title: CWE-32: Path Traversal: '...' (Triple Dot)
text:
CWE-32 Path Traversal: '...' (Triple Dot)
The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '...' (triple dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: '...' (Triple Dot)

title: CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
text:
CWE-321 Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
The product uses a hard-coded, unchangeable cryptographic key.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key

title: CWE-322: Key Exchange without Entity Authentication
text:
CWE-322 Key Exchange without Entity Authentication
The product performs a key exchange with an actor without verifying the identity of that actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Key Exchange without Entity Authentication

title: CWE-323: Reusing a Nonce, Key Pair in Encryption
text:
CWE-323 Reusing a Nonce, Key Pair in Encryption
Nonces should be used for the present occasion and only once.

metadata.cwe_name: Reusing a Nonce, Key Pair in Encryption

title: CWE-324: Use of a Key Past its Expiration Date
text:
CWE-324 Use of a Key Past its Expiration Date
The product uses a cryptographic key or password past its expiration date, which diminishes its safety significantly by increasing the timing window for cracking attacks against that key.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of a Key Past its Expiration Date

title: CWE-325: Missing Cryptographic Step
text:
CWE-325 Missing Cryptographic Step
The product does not implement a required step in a cryptographic algorithm, resulting in weaker encryption than advertised by the algorithm.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Cryptographic Step

title: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength
text:
CWE-326 Inadequate Encryption Strength
The product stores or transmits sensitive data using an encryption scheme that is theoretically sound, but is not strong enough for the level of protection required.

metadata.cwe_name: Inadequate Encryption Strength

title: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
text:
CWE-327 Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
The product uses a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm or protocol.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

title: CWE-328: Use of Weak Hash
text:
CWE-328 Use of Weak Hash
The product uses an algorithm that produces a digest (output value) that does not meet security expectations for a hash function that allows an adversary to reasonably determine the original input (preimage attack), find another input that can produce the same hash (2nd preimage attack), or find multiple inputs that evaluate to the same hash (birthday attack).

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Weak Hash

title: CWE-329: Generation of Predictable IV with CBC Mode
text:
CWE-329 Generation of Predictable IV with CBC Mode
The product generates and uses a predictable initialization Vector (IV) with Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) Mode, which causes algorithms to be susceptible to dictionary attacks when they are encrypted under the same key.

metadata.cwe_name: Generation of Predictable IV with CBC Mode

title: CWE-33: Path Traversal: '....' (Multiple Dot)
text:
CWE-33 Path Traversal: '....' (Multiple Dot)
The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '....' (multiple dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: '....' (Multiple Dot)

title: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
text:
CWE-330 Use of Insufficiently Random Values
The product uses insufficiently random numbers or values in a security context that depends on unpredictable numbers.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

title: CWE-331: Insufficient Entropy
text:
CWE-331 Insufficient Entropy
The product uses an algorithm or scheme that produces insufficient entropy, leaving patterns or clusters of values that are more likely to occur than others.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Entropy

title: CWE-332: Insufficient Entropy in PRNG
text:
CWE-332 Insufficient Entropy in PRNG
The lack of entropy available for, or used by, a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) can be a stability and security threat.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Entropy in PRNG

title: CWE-333: Improper Handling of Insufficient Entropy in TRNG
text:
CWE-333 Improper Handling of Insufficient Entropy in TRNG
True random number generators (TRNG) generally have a limited source of entropy and therefore can fail or block.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Insufficient Entropy in TRNG

title: CWE-334: Small Space of Random Values
text:
CWE-334 Small Space of Random Values
The number of possible random values is smaller than needed by the product, making it more susceptible to brute force attacks.

metadata.cwe_name: Small Space of Random Values

title: CWE-335: Incorrect Usage of Seeds in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
text:
CWE-335 Incorrect Usage of Seeds in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
The product uses a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) but does not correctly manage seeds.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Usage of Seeds in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

title: CWE-336: Same Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
text:
CWE-336 Same Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) uses the same seed each time the product is initialized.

metadata.cwe_name: Same Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

title: CWE-337: Predictable Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
text:
CWE-337 Predictable Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) is initialized from a predictable seed, such as the process ID or system time.

metadata.cwe_name: Predictable Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

title: CWE-338: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
text:
CWE-338 Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
The product uses a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) in a security context, but the PRNG's algorithm is not cryptographically strong.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

title: CWE-339: Small Seed Space in PRNG
text:
CWE-339 Small Seed Space in PRNG
A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) uses a relatively small seed space, which makes it more susceptible to brute force attacks.

metadata.cwe_name: Small Seed Space in PRNG

title: CWE-34: Path Traversal: '....//'
text:
CWE-34 Path Traversal: '....//'
The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '....//' (doubled dot dot slash) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: '....//'

title: CWE-340: Generation of Predictable Numbers or Identifiers
text:
CWE-340 Generation of Predictable Numbers or Identifiers
The product uses a scheme that generates numbers or identifiers that are more predictable than required.

metadata.cwe_name: Generation of Predictable Numbers or Identifiers

title: CWE-341: Predictable from Observable State
text:
CWE-341 Predictable from Observable State
A number or object is predictable based on observations that the attacker can make about the state of the system or network, such as time, process ID, etc.

metadata.cwe_name: Predictable from Observable State

title: CWE-342: Predictable Exact Value from Previous Values
text:
CWE-342 Predictable Exact Value from Previous Values
An exact value or random number can be precisely predicted by observing previous values.

metadata.cwe_name: Predictable Exact Value from Previous Values

title: CWE-343: Predictable Value Range from Previous Values
text:
CWE-343 Predictable Value Range from Previous Values
The product's random number generator produces a series of values which, when observed, can be used to infer a relatively small range of possibilities for the next value that could be generated.

metadata.cwe_name: Predictable Value Range from Previous Values

title: CWE-344: Use of Invariant Value in Dynamically Changing Context
text:
CWE-344 Use of Invariant Value in Dynamically Changing Context
The product uses a constant value, name, or reference, but this value can (or should) vary across different environments.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Invariant Value in Dynamically Changing Context

title: CWE-345: Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity
text:
CWE-345 Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity
The product does not sufficiently verify the origin or authenticity of data, in a way that causes it to accept invalid data.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity

title: CWE-346: Origin Validation Error
text:
CWE-346 Origin Validation Error
The product does not properly verify that the source of data or communication is valid.

metadata.cwe_name: Origin Validation Error

title: CWE-347: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
text:
CWE-347 Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
The product does not verify, or incorrectly verifies, the cryptographic signature for data.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature

title: CWE-348: Use of Less Trusted Source
text:
CWE-348 Use of Less Trusted Source
The product has two different sources of the same data or information, but it uses the source that has less support for verification, is less trusted, or is less resistant to attack.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Less Trusted Source

title: CWE-349: Acceptance of Extraneous Untrusted Data With Trusted Data
text:
CWE-349 Acceptance of Extraneous Untrusted Data With Trusted Data
The product, when processing trusted data, accepts any untrusted data that is also included with the trusted data, treating the untrusted data as if it were trusted.

metadata.cwe_name: Acceptance of Extraneous Untrusted Data With Trusted Data

title: CWE-35: Path Traversal: '.../...//'
text:
CWE-35 Path Traversal: '.../...//'
The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '.../...//' (doubled triple dot slash) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: '.../...//'

title: CWE-350: Reliance on Reverse DNS Resolution for a Security-Critical Action
text:
CWE-350 Reliance on Reverse DNS Resolution for a Security-Critical Action
The product performs reverse DNS resolution on an IP address to obtain the hostname and make a security decision, but it does not properly ensure that the IP address is truly associated with the hostname.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Reverse DNS Resolution for a Security-Critical Action

title: CWE-351: Insufficient Type Distinction
text:
CWE-351 Insufficient Type Distinction
The product does not properly distinguish between different types of elements in a way that leads to insecure behavior.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Type Distinction

title: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
text:
CWE-352 Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
The web application does not, or cannot, sufficiently verify whether a request was intentionally provided by the user who sent the request, which could have originated from an unauthorized actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

title: CWE-353: Missing Support for Integrity Check
text:
CWE-353 Missing Support for Integrity Check
The product uses a transmission protocol that does not include a mechanism for verifying the integrity of the data during transmission, such as a checksum.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Support for Integrity Check

title: CWE-354: Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value
text:
CWE-354 Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value
The product does not validate or incorrectly validates the integrity check values or "checksums" of a message. This may prevent it from detecting if the data has been modified or corrupted in transmission.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value

title: CWE-356: Product UI does not Warn User of Unsafe Actions
text:
CWE-356 Product UI does not Warn User of Unsafe Actions
The product's user interface does not warn the user before undertaking an unsafe action on behalf of that user. This makes it easier for attackers to trick users into inflicting damage to their system.

metadata.cwe_name: Product UI does not Warn User of Unsafe Actions

title: CWE-357: Insufficient UI Warning of Dangerous Operations
text:
CWE-357 Insufficient UI Warning of Dangerous Operations
The user interface provides a warning to a user regarding dangerous or sensitive operations, but the warning is not noticeable enough to warrant attention.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient UI Warning of Dangerous Operations

title: CWE-358: Improperly Implemented Security Check for Standard
text:
CWE-358 Improperly Implemented Security Check for Standard
The product does not implement or incorrectly implements one or more security-relevant checks as specified by the design of a standardized algorithm, protocol, or technique.

metadata.cwe_name: Improperly Implemented Security Check for Standard

title: CWE-359: Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor
text:
CWE-359 Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor
The product does not properly prevent a person's private, personal information from being accessed by actors who either (1) are not explicitly authorized to access the information or (2) do not have the implicit consent of the person about whom the information is collected.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor

title: CWE-36: Absolute Path Traversal
text:
CWE-36 Absolute Path Traversal
The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize absolute path sequences such as "/abs/path" that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

metadata.cwe_name: Absolute Path Traversal

title: CWE-360: Trust of System Event Data
text:
CWE-360 Trust of System Event Data
Security based on event locations are insecure and can be spoofed.

metadata.cwe_name: Trust of System Event Data

title: CWE-362: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
text:
CWE-362 Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
The product contains a concurrent code sequence that requires temporary, exclusive access to a shared resource, but a timing window exists in which the shared resource can be modified by another code sequence operating concurrently.

metadata.cwe_name: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')

title: CWE-363: Race Condition Enabling Link Following
text:
CWE-363 Race Condition Enabling Link Following
The product checks the status of a file or directory before accessing it, which produces a race condition in which the file can be replaced with a link before the access is performed, causing the product to access the wrong file.

metadata.cwe_name: Race Condition Enabling Link Following

title: CWE-364: Signal Handler Race Condition
text:
CWE-364 Signal Handler Race Condition
The product uses a signal handler that introduces a race condition.

metadata.cwe_name: Signal Handler Race Condition

title: CWE-365: DEPRECATED: Race Condition in Switch
text:
CWE-365 DEPRECATED: Race Condition in Switch
This entry has been deprecated. There are no documented cases in which a switch's control expression is evaluated more than once.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Race Condition in Switch

title: CWE-366: Race Condition within a Thread
text:
CWE-366 Race Condition within a Thread
If two threads of execution use a resource simultaneously, there exists the possibility that resources may be used while invalid, in turn making the state of execution undefined.

metadata.cwe_name: Race Condition within a Thread

title: CWE-367: Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition
text:
CWE-367 Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition
The product checks the state of a resource before using that resource, but the resource's state can change between the check and the use in a way that invalidates the results of the check.

metadata.cwe_name: Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition

title: CWE-368: Context Switching Race Condition
text:
CWE-368 Context Switching Race Condition
A product performs a series of non-atomic actions to switch between contexts that cross privilege or other security boundaries, but a race condition allows an attacker to modify or misrepresent the product's behavior during the switch.

metadata.cwe_name: Context Switching Race Condition

title: CWE-369: Divide By Zero
text:
CWE-369 Divide By Zero
The product divides a value by zero.

metadata.cwe_name: Divide By Zero

title: CWE-37: Path Traversal: '/absolute/pathname/here'
text:
CWE-37 Path Traversal: '/absolute/pathname/here'
The product accepts input in the form of a slash absolute path ('/absolute/pathname/here') without appropriate validation, which can allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: '/absolute/pathname/here'

title: CWE-370: Missing Check for Certificate Revocation after Initial Check
text:
CWE-370 Missing Check for Certificate Revocation after Initial Check
The product does not check the revocation status of a certificate after its initial revocation check, which can cause the product to perform privileged actions even after the certificate is revoked at a later time.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Check for Certificate Revocation after Initial Check

title: CWE-372: Incomplete Internal State Distinction
text:
CWE-372 Incomplete Internal State Distinction
The product does not properly determine which state it is in, causing it to assume it is in state X when in fact it is in state Y, causing it to perform incorrect operations in a security-relevant manner.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Internal State Distinction

title: CWE-373: DEPRECATED: State Synchronization Error
text:
CWE-373 DEPRECATED: State Synchronization Error
This entry was deprecated because it overlapped the same concepts as race condition (CWE-362) and Improper Synchronization (CWE-662).

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: State Synchronization Error

title: CWE-374: Passing Mutable Objects to an Untrusted Method
text:
CWE-374 Passing Mutable Objects to an Untrusted Method
The product sends non-cloned mutable data as an argument to a method or function.

metadata.cwe_name: Passing Mutable Objects to an Untrusted Method

title: CWE-375: Returning a Mutable Object to an Untrusted Caller
text:
CWE-375 Returning a Mutable Object to an Untrusted Caller
Sending non-cloned mutable data as a return value may result in that data being altered or deleted by the calling function.

metadata.cwe_name: Returning a Mutable Object to an Untrusted Caller

title: CWE-377: Insecure Temporary File
text:
CWE-377 Insecure Temporary File
Creating and using insecure temporary files can leave application and system data vulnerable to attack.

metadata.cwe_name: Insecure Temporary File

title: CWE-378: Creation of Temporary File With Insecure Permissions
text:
CWE-378 Creation of Temporary File With Insecure Permissions
Opening temporary files without appropriate measures or controls can leave the file, its contents and any function that it impacts vulnerable to attack.

metadata.cwe_name: Creation of Temporary File With Insecure Permissions

title: CWE-379: Creation of Temporary File in Directory with Insecure Permissions
text:
CWE-379 Creation of Temporary File in Directory with Insecure Permissions
The product creates a temporary file in a directory whose permissions allow unintended actors to determine the file's existence or otherwise access that file.

metadata.cwe_name: Creation of Temporary File in Directory with Insecure Permissions

title: CWE-38: Path Traversal: '\absolute\pathname\here'
text:
CWE-38 Path Traversal: '\absolute\pathname\here'
The product accepts input in the form of a backslash absolute path ('\absolute\pathname\here') without appropriate validation, which can allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: '\absolute\pathname\here'

title: CWE-382: J2EE Bad Practices: Use of System.exit()
text:
CWE-382 J2EE Bad Practices: Use of System.exit()
A J2EE application uses System.exit(), which also shuts down its container.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Bad Practices: Use of System.exit()

title: CWE-383: J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Use of Threads
text:
CWE-383 J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Use of Threads
Thread management in a Web application is forbidden in some circumstances and is always highly error prone.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Use of Threads

title: CWE-384: Session Fixation
text:
CWE-384 Session Fixation
Authenticating a user, or otherwise establishing a new user session, without invalidating any existing session identifier gives an attacker the opportunity to steal authenticated sessions.

metadata.cwe_name: Session Fixation

title: CWE-385: Covert Timing Channel
text:
CWE-385 Covert Timing Channel
Covert timing channels convey information by modulating some aspect of system behavior over time, so that the program receiving the information can observe system behavior and infer protected information.

metadata.cwe_name: Covert Timing Channel

title: CWE-386: Symbolic Name not Mapping to Correct Object
text:
CWE-386 Symbolic Name not Mapping to Correct Object
A constant symbolic reference to an object is used, even though the reference can resolve to a different object over time.

metadata.cwe_name: Symbolic Name not Mapping to Correct Object

title: CWE-39: Path Traversal: 'C:dirname'
text:
CWE-39 Path Traversal: 'C:dirname'
The product accepts input that contains a drive letter or Windows volume letter ('C:dirname') that potentially redirects access to an unintended location or arbitrary file.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: 'C:dirname'

title: CWE-390: Detection of Error Condition Without Action
text:
CWE-390 Detection of Error Condition Without Action
The product detects a specific error, but takes no actions to handle the error.

metadata.cwe_name: Detection of Error Condition Without Action

title: CWE-391: Unchecked Error Condition
text:
CWE-391 Unchecked Error Condition
[PLANNED FOR DEPRECATION. SEE MAINTENANCE NOTES AND CONSIDER CWE-252, CWE-248, OR CWE-1069.] Ignoring exceptions and other error conditions may allow an attacker to induce unexpected behavior unnoticed.

metadata.cwe_name: Unchecked Error Condition

title: CWE-392: Missing Report of Error Condition
text:
CWE-392 Missing Report of Error Condition
The product encounters an error but does not provide a status code or return value to indicate that an error has occurred.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Report of Error Condition

title: CWE-393: Return of Wrong Status Code
text:
CWE-393 Return of Wrong Status Code
A function or operation returns an incorrect return value or status code that does not indicate the true result of execution, causing the product to modify its behavior based on the incorrect result.

metadata.cwe_name: Return of Wrong Status Code

title: CWE-394: Unexpected Status Code or Return Value
text:
CWE-394 Unexpected Status Code or Return Value
The product does not properly check when a function or operation returns a value that is legitimate for the function, but is not expected by the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Unexpected Status Code or Return Value

title: CWE-395: Use of NullPointerException Catch to Detect NULL Pointer Dereference
text:
CWE-395 Use of NullPointerException Catch to Detect NULL Pointer Dereference
Catching NullPointerException should not be used as an alternative to programmatic checks to prevent dereferencing a null pointer.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of NullPointerException Catch to Detect NULL Pointer Dereference

title: CWE-396: Declaration of Catch for Generic Exception
text:
CWE-396 Declaration of Catch for Generic Exception
Catching overly broad exceptions promotes complex error handling code that is more likely to contain security vulnerabilities.

metadata.cwe_name: Declaration of Catch for Generic Exception

title: CWE-397: Declaration of Throws for Generic Exception
text:
CWE-397 Declaration of Throws for Generic Exception
The product throws or raises an overly broad exceptions that can hide important details and produce inappropriate responses to certain conditions.

metadata.cwe_name: Declaration of Throws for Generic Exception

title: CWE-40: Path Traversal: '\\UNC\share\name\' (Windows UNC Share)
text:
CWE-40 Path Traversal: '\\UNC\share\name\' (Windows UNC Share)
The product accepts input that identifies a Windows UNC share ('\\UNC\share\name') that potentially redirects access to an unintended location or arbitrary file.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Traversal: '\\UNC\share\name\' (Windows UNC Share)

title: CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
text:
CWE-400 Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
The product does not properly control the allocation and maintenance of a limited resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

title: CWE-401: Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
text:
CWE-401 Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
The product does not sufficiently track and release allocated memory after it has been used, making the memory unavailable for reallocation and reuse.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime

title: CWE-402: Transmission of Private Resources into a New Sphere ('Resource Leak')
text:
CWE-402 Transmission of Private Resources into a New Sphere ('Resource Leak')
The product makes resources available to untrusted parties when those resources are only intended to be accessed by the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Transmission of Private Resources into a New Sphere ('Resource Leak')

title: CWE-403: Exposure of File Descriptor to Unintended Control Sphere ('File Descriptor Leak')
text:
CWE-403 Exposure of File Descriptor to Unintended Control Sphere ('File Descriptor Leak')
A process does not close sensitive file descriptors before invoking a child process, which allows the child to perform unauthorized I/O operations using those descriptors.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of File Descriptor to Unintended Control Sphere ('File Descriptor Leak')

title: CWE-404: Improper Resource Shutdown or Release
text:
CWE-404 Improper Resource Shutdown or Release
The product does not release or incorrectly releases a resource before it is made available for re-use.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Resource Shutdown or Release

title: CWE-405: Asymmetric Resource Consumption (Amplification)
text:
CWE-405 Asymmetric Resource Consumption (Amplification)
The product does not properly control situations in which an adversary can cause the product to consume or produce excessive resources without requiring the adversary to invest equivalent work or otherwise prove authorization, i.e., the adversary's influence is "asymmetric."

metadata.cwe_name: Asymmetric Resource Consumption (Amplification)

title: CWE-406: Insufficient Control of Network Message Volume (Network Amplification)
text:
CWE-406 Insufficient Control of Network Message Volume (Network Amplification)
The product does not sufficiently monitor or control transmitted network traffic volume, so that an actor can cause the product to transmit more traffic than should be allowed for that actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Control of Network Message Volume (Network Amplification)

title: CWE-407: Inefficient Algorithmic Complexity
text:
CWE-407 Inefficient Algorithmic Complexity
An algorithm in a product has an inefficient worst-case computational complexity that may be detrimental to system performance and can be triggered by an attacker, typically using crafted manipulations that ensure that the worst case is being reached.

metadata.cwe_name: Inefficient Algorithmic Complexity

title: CWE-408: Incorrect Behavior Order: Early Amplification
text:
CWE-408 Incorrect Behavior Order: Early Amplification
The product allows an entity to perform a legitimate but expensive operation before authentication or authorization has taken place.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Behavior Order: Early Amplification

title: CWE-409: Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)
text:
CWE-409 Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)
The product does not handle or incorrectly handles a compressed input with a very high compression ratio that produces a large output.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)

title: CWE-41: Improper Resolution of Path Equivalence
text:
CWE-41 Improper Resolution of Path Equivalence
The product is vulnerable to file system contents disclosure through path equivalence. Path equivalence involves the use of special characters in file and directory names. The associated manipulations are intended to generate multiple names for the same object.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Resolution of Path Equivalence

title: CWE-410: Insufficient Resource Pool
text:
CWE-410 Insufficient Resource Pool
The product's resource pool is not large enough to handle peak demand, which allows an attacker to prevent others from accessing the resource by using a (relatively) large number of requests for resources.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Resource Pool

title: CWE-412: Unrestricted Externally Accessible Lock
text:
CWE-412 Unrestricted Externally Accessible Lock
The product properly checks for the existence of a lock, but the lock can be externally controlled or influenced by an actor that is outside of the intended sphere of control.

metadata.cwe_name: Unrestricted Externally Accessible Lock

title: CWE-413: Improper Resource Locking
text:
CWE-413 Improper Resource Locking
The product does not lock or does not correctly lock a resource when the product must have exclusive access to the resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Resource Locking

title: CWE-414: Missing Lock Check
text:
CWE-414 Missing Lock Check
A product does not check to see if a lock is present before performing sensitive operations on a resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Lock Check

title: CWE-415: Double Free
text:
CWE-415 Double Free
The product calls free() twice on the same memory address.

metadata.cwe_name: Double Free

title: CWE-416: Use After Free
text:
CWE-416 Use After Free
The product reuses or references memory after it has been freed. At some point afterward, the memory may be allocated again and saved in another pointer, while the original pointer references a location somewhere within the new allocation. Any operations using the original pointer are no longer valid because the memory "belongs" to the code that operates on the new pointer.

metadata.cwe_name: Use After Free

title: CWE-419: Unprotected Primary Channel
text:
CWE-419 Unprotected Primary Channel
The product uses a primary channel for administration or restricted functionality, but it does not properly protect the channel.

metadata.cwe_name: Unprotected Primary Channel

title: CWE-42: Path Equivalence: 'filename.' (Trailing Dot)
text:
CWE-42 Path Equivalence: 'filename.' (Trailing Dot)
The product accepts path input in the form of trailing dot ('filedir.') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'filename.' (Trailing Dot)

title: CWE-420: Unprotected Alternate Channel
text:
CWE-420 Unprotected Alternate Channel
The product protects a primary channel, but it does not use the same level of protection for an alternate channel.

metadata.cwe_name: Unprotected Alternate Channel

title: CWE-421: Race Condition During Access to Alternate Channel
text:
CWE-421 Race Condition During Access to Alternate Channel
The product opens an alternate channel to communicate with an authorized user, but the channel is accessible to other actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Race Condition During Access to Alternate Channel

title: CWE-422: Unprotected Windows Messaging Channel ('Shatter')
text:
CWE-422 Unprotected Windows Messaging Channel ('Shatter')
The product does not properly verify the source of a message in the Windows Messaging System while running at elevated privileges, creating an alternate channel through which an attacker can directly send a message to the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Unprotected Windows Messaging Channel ('Shatter')

title: CWE-423: DEPRECATED: Proxied Trusted Channel
text:
CWE-423 DEPRECATED: Proxied Trusted Channel
This entry has been deprecated because it was a duplicate of CWE-441. All content has been transferred to CWE-441.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Proxied Trusted Channel

title: CWE-424: Improper Protection of Alternate Path
text:
CWE-424 Improper Protection of Alternate Path
The product does not sufficiently protect all possible paths that a user can take to access restricted functionality or resources.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Protection of Alternate Path

title: CWE-425: Direct Request ('Forced Browsing')
text:
CWE-425 Direct Request ('Forced Browsing')
The web application does not adequately enforce appropriate authorization on all restricted URLs, scripts, or files.

metadata.cwe_name: Direct Request ('Forced Browsing')

title: CWE-426: Untrusted Search Path
text:
CWE-426 Untrusted Search Path
The product searches for critical resources using an externally-supplied search path that can point to resources that are not under the product's direct control.

metadata.cwe_name: Untrusted Search Path

title: CWE-427: Uncontrolled Search Path Element
text:
CWE-427 Uncontrolled Search Path Element
The product uses a fixed or controlled search path to find resources, but one or more locations in that path can be under the control of unintended actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Uncontrolled Search Path Element

title: CWE-428: Unquoted Search Path or Element
text:
CWE-428 Unquoted Search Path or Element
The product uses a search path that contains an unquoted element, in which the element contains whitespace or other separators. This can cause the product to access resources in a parent path.

metadata.cwe_name: Unquoted Search Path or Element

title: CWE-43: Path Equivalence: 'filename....' (Multiple Trailing Dot)
text:
CWE-43 Path Equivalence: 'filename....' (Multiple Trailing Dot)
The product accepts path input in the form of multiple trailing dot ('filedir....') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'filename....' (Multiple Trailing Dot)

title: CWE-430: Deployment of Wrong Handler
text:
CWE-430 Deployment of Wrong Handler
The wrong "handler" is assigned to process an object.

metadata.cwe_name: Deployment of Wrong Handler

title: CWE-431: Missing Handler
text:
CWE-431 Missing Handler
A handler is not available or implemented.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Handler

title: CWE-432: Dangerous Signal Handler not Disabled During Sensitive Operations
text:
CWE-432 Dangerous Signal Handler not Disabled During Sensitive Operations
The product uses a signal handler that shares state with other signal handlers, but it does not properly mask or prevent those signal handlers from being invoked while the original signal handler is still running.

metadata.cwe_name: Dangerous Signal Handler not Disabled During Sensitive Operations

title: CWE-433: Unparsed Raw Web Content Delivery
text:
CWE-433 Unparsed Raw Web Content Delivery
The product stores raw content or supporting code under the web document root with an extension that is not specifically handled by the server.

metadata.cwe_name: Unparsed Raw Web Content Delivery

title: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
text:
CWE-434 Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.

metadata.cwe_name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type

title: CWE-435: Improper Interaction Between Multiple Correctly-Behaving Entities
text:
CWE-435 Improper Interaction Between Multiple Correctly-Behaving Entities
An interaction error occurs when two entities have correct behavior when running independently of each other, but when they are integrated as components in a larger system or process, they introduce incorrect behaviors that may cause resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Interaction Between Multiple Correctly-Behaving Entities

title: CWE-436: Interpretation Conflict
text:
CWE-436 Interpretation Conflict
Product A handles inputs or steps differently than Product B, which causes A to perform incorrect actions based on its perception of B's state.

metadata.cwe_name: Interpretation Conflict

title: CWE-437: Incomplete Model of Endpoint Features
text:
CWE-437 Incomplete Model of Endpoint Features
A product acts as an intermediary or monitor between two or more endpoints, but it does not have a complete model of an endpoint's features, behaviors, or state, potentially causing the product to perform incorrect actions based on this incomplete model.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Model of Endpoint Features

title: CWE-439: Behavioral Change in New Version or Environment
text:
CWE-439 Behavioral Change in New Version or Environment
A's behavior or functionality changes with a new version of A, or a new environment, which is not known (or manageable) by B.

metadata.cwe_name: Behavioral Change in New Version or Environment

title: CWE-44: Path Equivalence: 'file.name' (Internal Dot)
text:
CWE-44 Path Equivalence: 'file.name' (Internal Dot)
The product accepts path input in the form of internal dot ('file.ordir') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'file.name' (Internal Dot)

title: CWE-440: Expected Behavior Violation
text:
CWE-440 Expected Behavior Violation
A feature, API, or function does not perform according to its specification.

metadata.cwe_name: Expected Behavior Violation

title: CWE-441: Unintended Proxy or Intermediary ('Confused Deputy')
text:
CWE-441 Unintended Proxy or Intermediary ('Confused Deputy')
The product receives a request, message, or directive from an upstream component, but the product does not sufficiently preserve the original source of the request before forwarding the request to an external actor that is outside of the product's control sphere. This causes the product to appear to be the source of the request, leading it to act as a proxy or other intermediary between the upstream component and the external actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Unintended Proxy or Intermediary ('Confused Deputy')

title: CWE-443: DEPRECATED: HTTP response splitting
text:
CWE-443 DEPRECATED: HTTP response splitting
This weakness can be found at CWE-113.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: HTTP response splitting

title: CWE-444: Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests ('HTTP Request/Response Smuggling')
text:
CWE-444 Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests ('HTTP Request/Response Smuggling')
The product acts as an intermediary HTTP agent
 (such as a proxy or firewall) in the data flow between two
 entities such as a client and server, but it does not
 interpret malformed HTTP requests or responses in ways that
 are consistent with how the messages will be processed by
 those entities that are at the ultimate destination.

metadata.cwe_name: Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests ('HTTP Request/Response Smuggling')

title: CWE-446: UI Discrepancy for Security Feature
text:
CWE-446 UI Discrepancy for Security Feature
The user interface does not correctly enable or configure a security feature, but the interface provides feedback that causes the user to believe that the feature is in a secure state.

metadata.cwe_name: UI Discrepancy for Security Feature

title: CWE-447: Unimplemented or Unsupported Feature in UI
text:
CWE-447 Unimplemented or Unsupported Feature in UI
A UI function for a security feature appears to be supported and gives feedback to the user that suggests that it is supported, but the underlying functionality is not implemented.

metadata.cwe_name: Unimplemented or Unsupported Feature in UI

title: CWE-448: Obsolete Feature in UI
text:
CWE-448 Obsolete Feature in UI
A UI function is obsolete and the product does not warn the user.

metadata.cwe_name: Obsolete Feature in UI

title: CWE-449: The UI Performs the Wrong Action
text:
CWE-449 The UI Performs the Wrong Action
The UI performs the wrong action with respect to the user's request.

metadata.cwe_name: The UI Performs the Wrong Action

title: CWE-45: Path Equivalence: 'file...name' (Multiple Internal Dot)
text:
CWE-45 Path Equivalence: 'file...name' (Multiple Internal Dot)
The product accepts path input in the form of multiple internal dot ('file...dir') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'file...name' (Multiple Internal Dot)

title: CWE-450: Multiple Interpretations of UI Input
text:
CWE-450 Multiple Interpretations of UI Input
The UI has multiple interpretations of user input but does not prompt the user when it selects the less secure interpretation.

metadata.cwe_name: Multiple Interpretations of UI Input

title: CWE-451: User Interface (UI) Misrepresentation of Critical Information
text:
CWE-451 User Interface (UI) Misrepresentation of Critical Information
The user interface (UI) does not properly represent critical information to the user, allowing the information - or its source - to be obscured or spoofed. This is often a component in phishing attacks.

metadata.cwe_name: User Interface (UI) Misrepresentation of Critical Information

title: CWE-453: Insecure Default Variable Initialization
text:
CWE-453 Insecure Default Variable Initialization
The product, by default, initializes an internal variable with an insecure or less secure value than is possible.

metadata.cwe_name: Insecure Default Variable Initialization

title: CWE-454: External Initialization of Trusted Variables or Data Stores
text:
CWE-454 External Initialization of Trusted Variables or Data Stores
The product initializes critical internal variables or data stores using inputs that can be modified by untrusted actors.

metadata.cwe_name: External Initialization of Trusted Variables or Data Stores

title: CWE-455: Non-exit on Failed Initialization
text:
CWE-455 Non-exit on Failed Initialization
The product does not exit or otherwise modify its operation when security-relevant errors occur during initialization, such as when a configuration file has a format error or a hardware security module (HSM) cannot be activated, which can cause the product to execute in a less secure fashion than intended by the administrator.

metadata.cwe_name: Non-exit on Failed Initialization

title: CWE-456: Missing Initialization of a Variable
text:
CWE-456 Missing Initialization of a Variable
The product does not initialize critical variables, which causes the execution environment to use unexpected values.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Initialization of a Variable

title: CWE-457: Use of Uninitialized Variable
text:
CWE-457 Use of Uninitialized Variable
The code uses a variable that has not been initialized, leading to unpredictable or unintended results.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Uninitialized Variable

title: CWE-458: DEPRECATED: Incorrect Initialization
text:
CWE-458 DEPRECATED: Incorrect Initialization
This weakness has been deprecated because its name and description did not match. The description duplicated CWE-454, while the name suggested a more abstract initialization problem. Please refer to CWE-665 for the more abstract problem.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Incorrect Initialization

title: CWE-459: Incomplete Cleanup
text:
CWE-459 Incomplete Cleanup
The product does not properly "clean up" and remove temporary or supporting resources after they have been used.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Cleanup

title: CWE-46: Path Equivalence: 'filename ' (Trailing Space)
text:
CWE-46 Path Equivalence: 'filename ' (Trailing Space)
The product accepts path input in the form of trailing space ('filedir ') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'filename ' (Trailing Space)

title: CWE-460: Improper Cleanup on Thrown Exception
text:
CWE-460 Improper Cleanup on Thrown Exception
The product does not clean up its state or incorrectly cleans up its state when an exception is thrown, leading to unexpected state or control flow.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Cleanup on Thrown Exception

title: CWE-462: Duplicate Key in Associative List (Alist)
text:
CWE-462 Duplicate Key in Associative List (Alist)
Duplicate keys in associative lists can lead to non-unique keys being mistaken for an error.

metadata.cwe_name: Duplicate Key in Associative List (Alist)

title: CWE-463: Deletion of Data Structure Sentinel
text:
CWE-463 Deletion of Data Structure Sentinel
The accidental deletion of a data-structure sentinel can cause serious programming logic problems.

metadata.cwe_name: Deletion of Data Structure Sentinel

title: CWE-464: Addition of Data Structure Sentinel
text:
CWE-464 Addition of Data Structure Sentinel
The accidental addition of a data-structure sentinel can cause serious programming logic problems.

metadata.cwe_name: Addition of Data Structure Sentinel

title: CWE-466: Return of Pointer Value Outside of Expected Range
text:
CWE-466 Return of Pointer Value Outside of Expected Range
A function can return a pointer to memory that is outside of the buffer that the pointer is expected to reference.

metadata.cwe_name: Return of Pointer Value Outside of Expected Range

title: CWE-467: Use of sizeof() on a Pointer Type
text:
CWE-467 Use of sizeof() on a Pointer Type
The code calls sizeof() on a pointer type, which can be an incorrect calculation if the programmer intended to determine the size of the data that is being pointed to.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of sizeof() on a Pointer Type

title: CWE-468: Incorrect Pointer Scaling
text:
CWE-468 Incorrect Pointer Scaling
In C and C++, one may often accidentally refer to the wrong memory due to the semantics of when math operations are implicitly scaled.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Pointer Scaling

title: CWE-469: Use of Pointer Subtraction to Determine Size
text:
CWE-469 Use of Pointer Subtraction to Determine Size
The product subtracts one pointer from another in order to determine size, but this calculation can be incorrect if the pointers do not exist in the same memory chunk.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Pointer Subtraction to Determine Size

title: CWE-47: Path Equivalence: ' filename' (Leading Space)
text:
CWE-47 Path Equivalence: ' filename' (Leading Space)
The product accepts path input in the form of leading space (' filedir') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: ' filename' (Leading Space)

title: CWE-470: Use of Externally-Controlled Input to Select Classes or Code ('Unsafe Reflection')
text:
CWE-470 Use of Externally-Controlled Input to Select Classes or Code ('Unsafe Reflection')
The product uses external input with reflection to select which classes or code to use, but it does not sufficiently prevent the input from selecting improper classes or code.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Externally-Controlled Input to Select Classes or Code ('Unsafe Reflection')

title: CWE-471: Modification of Assumed-Immutable Data (MAID)
text:
CWE-471 Modification of Assumed-Immutable Data (MAID)
The product does not properly protect an assumed-immutable element from being modified by an attacker.

metadata.cwe_name: Modification of Assumed-Immutable Data (MAID)

title: CWE-472: External Control of Assumed-Immutable Web Parameter
text:
CWE-472 External Control of Assumed-Immutable Web Parameter
The web application does not sufficiently verify inputs that are assumed to be immutable but are actually externally controllable, such as hidden form fields.

metadata.cwe_name: External Control of Assumed-Immutable Web Parameter

title: CWE-473: PHP External Variable Modification
text:
CWE-473 PHP External Variable Modification
A PHP application does not properly protect against the modification of variables from external sources, such as query parameters or cookies. This can expose the application to numerous weaknesses that would not exist otherwise.

metadata.cwe_name: PHP External Variable Modification

title: CWE-474: Use of Function with Inconsistent Implementations
text:
CWE-474 Use of Function with Inconsistent Implementations
The code uses a function that has inconsistent implementations across operating systems and versions.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Function with Inconsistent Implementations

title: CWE-475: Undefined Behavior for Input to API
text:
CWE-475 Undefined Behavior for Input to API
The behavior of this function is undefined unless its control parameter is set to a specific value.

metadata.cwe_name: Undefined Behavior for Input to API

title: CWE-476: NULL Pointer Dereference
text:
CWE-476 NULL Pointer Dereference
The product dereferences a pointer that it expects to be valid but is NULL.

metadata.cwe_name: NULL Pointer Dereference

title: CWE-477: Use of Obsolete Function
text:
CWE-477 Use of Obsolete Function
The code uses deprecated or obsolete functions, which suggests that the code has not been actively reviewed or maintained.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Obsolete Function

title: CWE-478: Missing Default Case in Multiple Condition Expression
text:
CWE-478 Missing Default Case in Multiple Condition Expression
The code does not have a default case in an expression with multiple conditions, such as a switch statement.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Default Case in Multiple Condition Expression

title: CWE-479: Signal Handler Use of a Non-reentrant Function
text:
CWE-479 Signal Handler Use of a Non-reentrant Function
The product defines a signal handler that calls a non-reentrant function.

metadata.cwe_name: Signal Handler Use of a Non-reentrant Function

title: CWE-48: Path Equivalence: 'file name' (Internal Whitespace)
text:
CWE-48 Path Equivalence: 'file name' (Internal Whitespace)
The product accepts path input in the form of internal space ('file(SPACE)name') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'file name' (Internal Whitespace)

title: CWE-480: Use of Incorrect Operator
text:
CWE-480 Use of Incorrect Operator
The product accidentally uses the wrong operator, which changes the logic in security-relevant ways.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Incorrect Operator

title: CWE-481: Assigning instead of Comparing
text:
CWE-481 Assigning instead of Comparing
The code uses an operator for assignment when the intention was to perform a comparison.

metadata.cwe_name: Assigning instead of Comparing

title: CWE-482: Comparing instead of Assigning
text:
CWE-482 Comparing instead of Assigning
The code uses an operator for comparison when the intention was to perform an assignment.

metadata.cwe_name: Comparing instead of Assigning

title: CWE-483: Incorrect Block Delimitation
text:
CWE-483 Incorrect Block Delimitation
The code does not explicitly delimit a block that is intended to contain 2 or more statements, creating a logic error.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Block Delimitation

title: CWE-484: Omitted Break Statement in Switch
text:
CWE-484 Omitted Break Statement in Switch
The product omits a break statement within a switch or similar construct, causing code associated with multiple conditions to execute. This can cause problems when the programmer only intended to execute code associated with one condition.

metadata.cwe_name: Omitted Break Statement in Switch

title: CWE-486: Comparison of Classes by Name
text:
CWE-486 Comparison of Classes by Name
The product compares classes by name, which can cause it to use the wrong class when multiple classes can have the same name.

metadata.cwe_name: Comparison of Classes by Name

title: CWE-487: Reliance on Package-level Scope
text:
CWE-487 Reliance on Package-level Scope
Java packages are not inherently closed; therefore, relying on them for code security is not a good practice.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Package-level Scope

title: CWE-488: Exposure of Data Element to Wrong Session
text:
CWE-488 Exposure of Data Element to Wrong Session
The product does not sufficiently enforce boundaries between the states of different sessions, causing data to be provided to, or used by, the wrong session.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Data Element to Wrong Session

title: CWE-489: Active Debug Code
text:
CWE-489 Active Debug Code
The product is released with debugging code still enabled or active.

metadata.cwe_name: Active Debug Code

title: CWE-49: Path Equivalence: 'filename/' (Trailing Slash)
text:
CWE-49 Path Equivalence: 'filename/' (Trailing Slash)
The product accepts path input in the form of trailing slash ('filedir/') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'filename/' (Trailing Slash)

title: CWE-491: Public cloneable() Method Without Final ('Object Hijack')
text:
CWE-491 Public cloneable() Method Without Final ('Object Hijack')
A class has a cloneable() method that is not declared final, which allows an object to be created without calling the constructor. This can cause the object to be in an unexpected state.

metadata.cwe_name: Public cloneable() Method Without Final ('Object Hijack')

title: CWE-492: Use of Inner Class Containing Sensitive Data
text:
CWE-492 Use of Inner Class Containing Sensitive Data
Inner classes are translated into classes that are accessible at package scope and may expose code that the programmer intended to keep private to attackers.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Inner Class Containing Sensitive Data

title: CWE-493: Critical Public Variable Without Final Modifier
text:
CWE-493 Critical Public Variable Without Final Modifier
The product has a critical public variable that is not final, which allows the variable to be modified to contain unexpected values.

metadata.cwe_name: Critical Public Variable Without Final Modifier

title: CWE-494: Download of Code Without Integrity Check
text:
CWE-494 Download of Code Without Integrity Check
The product downloads source code or an executable from a remote location and executes the code without sufficiently verifying the origin and integrity of the code.

metadata.cwe_name: Download of Code Without Integrity Check

title: CWE-495: Private Data Structure Returned From A Public Method
text:
CWE-495 Private Data Structure Returned From A Public Method
The product has a method that is declared public, but returns a reference to a private data structure, which could then be modified in unexpected ways.

metadata.cwe_name: Private Data Structure Returned From A Public Method

title: CWE-496: Public Data Assigned to Private Array-Typed Field
text:
CWE-496 Public Data Assigned to Private Array-Typed Field
Assigning public data to a private array is equivalent to giving public access to the array.

metadata.cwe_name: Public Data Assigned to Private Array-Typed Field

title: CWE-497: Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
text:
CWE-497 Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
The product does not properly prevent sensitive system-level information from being accessed by unauthorized actors who do not have the same level of access to the underlying system as the product does.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere

title: CWE-498: Cloneable Class Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-498 Cloneable Class Containing Sensitive Information
The code contains a class with sensitive data, but the class is cloneable. The data can then be accessed by cloning the class.

metadata.cwe_name: Cloneable Class Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-499: Serializable Class Containing Sensitive Data
text:
CWE-499 Serializable Class Containing Sensitive Data
The code contains a class with sensitive data, but the class does not explicitly deny serialization. The data can be accessed by serializing the class through another class.

metadata.cwe_name: Serializable Class Containing Sensitive Data

title: CWE-5: J2EE Misconfiguration: Data Transmission Without Encryption
text:
CWE-5 J2EE Misconfiguration: Data Transmission Without Encryption
Information sent over a network can be compromised while in transit. An attacker may be able to read or modify the contents if the data are sent in plaintext or are weakly encrypted.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Data Transmission Without Encryption

title: CWE-50: Path Equivalence: '//multiple/leading/slash'
text:
CWE-50 Path Equivalence: '//multiple/leading/slash'
The product accepts path input in the form of multiple leading slash ('//multiple/leading/slash') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: '//multiple/leading/slash'

title: CWE-500: Public Static Field Not Marked Final
text:
CWE-500 Public Static Field Not Marked Final
An object contains a public static field that is not marked final, which might allow it to be modified in unexpected ways.

metadata.cwe_name: Public Static Field Not Marked Final

title: CWE-501: Trust Boundary Violation
text:
CWE-501 Trust Boundary Violation
The product mixes trusted and untrusted data in the same data structure or structured message.

metadata.cwe_name: Trust Boundary Violation

title: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
text:
CWE-502 Deserialization of Untrusted Data
The product deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently ensuring that the resulting data will be valid.

metadata.cwe_name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

title: CWE-506: Embedded Malicious Code
text:
CWE-506 Embedded Malicious Code
The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.

metadata.cwe_name: Embedded Malicious Code

title: CWE-507: Trojan Horse
text:
CWE-507 Trojan Horse
The product appears to contain benign or useful functionality, but it also contains code that is hidden from normal operation that violates the intended security policy of the user or the system administrator.

metadata.cwe_name: Trojan Horse

title: CWE-508: Non-Replicating Malicious Code
text:
CWE-508 Non-Replicating Malicious Code
Non-replicating malicious code only resides on the target system or product that is attacked; it does not attempt to spread to other systems.

metadata.cwe_name: Non-Replicating Malicious Code

title: CWE-509: Replicating Malicious Code (Virus or Worm)
text:
CWE-509 Replicating Malicious Code (Virus or Worm)
Replicating malicious code, including viruses and worms, will attempt to attack other systems once it has successfully compromised the target system or the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Replicating Malicious Code (Virus or Worm)

title: CWE-51: Path Equivalence: '/multiple//internal/slash'
text:
CWE-51 Path Equivalence: '/multiple//internal/slash'
The product accepts path input in the form of multiple internal slash ('/multiple//internal/slash/') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: '/multiple//internal/slash'

title: CWE-510: Trapdoor
text:
CWE-510 Trapdoor
A trapdoor is a hidden piece of code that responds to a special input, allowing its user access to resources without passing through the normal security enforcement mechanism.

metadata.cwe_name: Trapdoor

title: CWE-511: Logic/Time Bomb
text:
CWE-511 Logic/Time Bomb
The product contains code that is designed to disrupt the legitimate operation of the product (or its environment) when a certain time passes, or when a certain logical condition is met.

metadata.cwe_name: Logic/Time Bomb

title: CWE-512: Spyware
text:
CWE-512 Spyware
The product collects personally identifiable information about a human user or the user's activities, but the product accesses this information using other resources besides itself, and it does not require that user's explicit approval or direct input into the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Spyware

title: CWE-514: Covert Channel
text:
CWE-514 Covert Channel
A covert channel is a path that can be used to transfer information in a way not intended by the system's designers.

metadata.cwe_name: Covert Channel

title: CWE-515: Covert Storage Channel
text:
CWE-515 Covert Storage Channel
A covert storage channel transfers information through the setting of bits by one program and the reading of those bits by another. What distinguishes this case from that of ordinary operation is that the bits are used to convey encoded information.

metadata.cwe_name: Covert Storage Channel

title: CWE-516: DEPRECATED: Covert Timing Channel
text:
CWE-516 DEPRECATED: Covert Timing Channel
This weakness can be found at CWE-385.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Covert Timing Channel

title: CWE-52: Path Equivalence: '/multiple/trailing/slash//'
text:
CWE-52 Path Equivalence: '/multiple/trailing/slash//'
The product accepts path input in the form of multiple trailing slash ('/multiple/trailing/slash//') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: '/multiple/trailing/slash//'

title: CWE-520: .NET Misconfiguration: Use of Impersonation
text:
CWE-520 .NET Misconfiguration: Use of Impersonation
Allowing a .NET application to run at potentially escalated levels of access to the underlying operating and file systems can be dangerous and result in various forms of attacks.

metadata.cwe_name: .NET Misconfiguration: Use of Impersonation

title: CWE-521: Weak Password Requirements
text:
CWE-521 Weak Password Requirements
The product does not require that users should have strong passwords.

metadata.cwe_name: Weak Password Requirements

title: CWE-522: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
text:
CWE-522 Insufficiently Protected Credentials
The product transmits or stores authentication credentials, but it uses an insecure method that is susceptible to unauthorized interception and/or retrieval.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficiently Protected Credentials

title: CWE-523: Unprotected Transport of Credentials
text:
CWE-523 Unprotected Transport of Credentials
Login pages do not use adequate measures to protect the user name and password while they are in transit from the client to the server.

metadata.cwe_name: Unprotected Transport of Credentials

title: CWE-524: Use of Cache Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-524 Use of Cache Containing Sensitive Information
The code uses a cache that contains sensitive information, but the cache can be read by an actor outside of the intended control sphere.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Cache Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-525: Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-525 Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information
The web application does not use an appropriate caching policy that specifies the extent to which each web page and associated form fields should be cached.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-526: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in an Environment Variable
text:
CWE-526 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in an Environment Variable
The product uses an environment variable to store unencrypted sensitive information.

metadata.cwe_name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in an Environment Variable

title: CWE-527: Exposure of Version-Control Repository to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
text:
CWE-527 Exposure of Version-Control Repository to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
The product stores a CVS, git, or other repository in a directory, archive, or other resource that is stored, transferred, or otherwise made accessible to unauthorized actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Version-Control Repository to an Unauthorized Control Sphere

title: CWE-528: Exposure of Core Dump File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
text:
CWE-528 Exposure of Core Dump File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
The product generates a core dump file in a directory, archive, or other resource that is stored, transferred, or otherwise made accessible to unauthorized actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Core Dump File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere

title: CWE-529: Exposure of Access Control List Files to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
text:
CWE-529 Exposure of Access Control List Files to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
The product stores access control list files in a directory or other container that is accessible to actors outside of the intended control sphere.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Access Control List Files to an Unauthorized Control Sphere

title: CWE-53: Path Equivalence: '\multiple\\internal\backslash'
text:
CWE-53 Path Equivalence: '\multiple\\internal\backslash'
The product accepts path input in the form of multiple internal backslash ('\multiple\trailing\\slash') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: '\multiple\\internal\backslash'

title: CWE-530: Exposure of Backup File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
text:
CWE-530 Exposure of Backup File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
A backup file is stored in a directory or archive that is made accessible to unauthorized actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Backup File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere

title: CWE-531: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Test Code
text:
CWE-531 Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Test Code
Accessible test applications can pose a variety of security risks. Since developers or administrators rarely consider that someone besides themselves would even know about the existence of these applications, it is common for them to contain sensitive information or functions.

metadata.cwe_name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Test Code

title: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
text:
CWE-532 Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

metadata.cwe_name: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

text:

title: CWE-535: Exposure of Information Through Shell Error Message
text:
CWE-535 Exposure of Information Through Shell Error Message
A command shell error message indicates that there exists an unhandled exception in the web application code. In many cases, an attacker can leverage the conditions that cause these errors in order to gain unauthorized access to the system.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Information Through Shell Error Message

title: CWE-536: Servlet Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-536 Servlet Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
A servlet error message indicates that there exists an unhandled exception in the web application code and may provide useful information to an attacker.

metadata.cwe_name: Servlet Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-537: Java Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-537 Java Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
In many cases, an attacker can leverage the conditions that cause unhandled exception errors in order to gain unauthorized access to the system.

metadata.cwe_name: Java Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-538: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Externally-Accessible File or Directory
text:
CWE-538 Insertion of Sensitive Information into Externally-Accessible File or Directory
The product places sensitive information into files or directories that are accessible to actors who are allowed to have access to the files, but not to the sensitive information.

metadata.cwe_name: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Externally-Accessible File or Directory

title: CWE-539: Use of Persistent Cookies Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-539 Use of Persistent Cookies Containing Sensitive Information
The web application uses persistent cookies, but the cookies contain sensitive information.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Persistent Cookies Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-54: Path Equivalence: 'filedir\' (Trailing Backslash)
text:
