metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'filedir\' (Trailing Backslash)

title: CWE-540: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code
text:
CWE-540 Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code
Source code on a web server or repository often contains sensitive information and should generally not be accessible to users.

metadata.cwe_name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code

title: CWE-541: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in an Include File
text:
CWE-541 Inclusion of Sensitive Information in an Include File
If an include file source is accessible, the file can contain usernames and passwords, as well as sensitive information pertaining to the application and system.

metadata.cwe_name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in an Include File

title: CWE-542: DEPRECATED: Information Exposure Through Cleanup Log Files
text:
CWE-542 DEPRECATED: Information Exposure Through Cleanup Log Files
This entry has been deprecated because its abstraction was too low-level. See CWE-532.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Information Exposure Through Cleanup Log Files

title: CWE-543: Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context
text:
CWE-543 Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context
The product uses the singleton pattern when creating a resource within a multithreaded environment.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context

title: CWE-544: Missing Standardized Error Handling Mechanism
text:
CWE-544 Missing Standardized Error Handling Mechanism
The product does not use a standardized method for handling errors throughout the code, which might introduce inconsistent error handling and resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Standardized Error Handling Mechanism

title: CWE-545: DEPRECATED: Use of Dynamic Class Loading
text:
CWE-545 DEPRECATED: Use of Dynamic Class Loading
This weakness has been deprecated because it partially overlaps CWE-470, it describes legitimate programmer behavior, and other portions will need to be integrated into other entries.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Use of Dynamic Class Loading

title: CWE-546: Suspicious Comment
text:
CWE-546 Suspicious Comment
The code contains comments that suggest the presence of bugs, incomplete functionality, or weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Suspicious Comment

title: CWE-547: Use of Hard-coded, Security-relevant Constants
text:
CWE-547 Use of Hard-coded, Security-relevant Constants
The product uses hard-coded constants instead of symbolic names for security-critical values, which increases the likelihood of mistakes during code maintenance or security policy change.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Hard-coded, Security-relevant Constants

title: CWE-548: Exposure of Information Through Directory Listing
text:
CWE-548 Exposure of Information Through Directory Listing
The product inappropriately exposes a directory listing with an index of all the resources located inside of the directory.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Information Through Directory Listing

title: CWE-549: Missing Password Field Masking
text:
CWE-549 Missing Password Field Masking
The product does not mask passwords during entry, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture passwords.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Password Field Masking

title: CWE-55: Path Equivalence: '/./' (Single Dot Directory)
text:
CWE-55 Path Equivalence: '/./' (Single Dot Directory)
The product accepts path input in the form of single dot directory exploit ('/./') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: '/./' (Single Dot Directory)

title: CWE-550: Server-generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-550 Server-generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
Certain conditions, such as network failure, will cause a server error message to be displayed.

metadata.cwe_name: Server-generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-551: Incorrect Behavior Order: Authorization Before Parsing and Canonicalization
text:
CWE-551 Incorrect Behavior Order: Authorization Before Parsing and Canonicalization
If a web server does not fully parse requested URLs before it examines them for authorization, it may be possible for an attacker to bypass authorization protection.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Behavior Order: Authorization Before Parsing and Canonicalization

title: CWE-552: Files or Directories Accessible to External Parties
text:
CWE-552 Files or Directories Accessible to External Parties
The product makes files or directories accessible to unauthorized actors, even though they should not be.

metadata.cwe_name: Files or Directories Accessible to External Parties

title: CWE-553: Command Shell in Externally Accessible Directory
text:
CWE-553 Command Shell in Externally Accessible Directory
A possible shell file exists in /cgi-bin/ or other accessible directories. This is extremely dangerous and can be used by an attacker to execute commands on the web server.

metadata.cwe_name: Command Shell in Externally Accessible Directory

title: CWE-554: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Not Using Input Validation Framework
text:
CWE-554 ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Not Using Input Validation Framework
The ASP.NET application does not use an input validation framework.

metadata.cwe_name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Not Using Input Validation Framework

title: CWE-555: J2EE Misconfiguration: Plaintext Password in Configuration File
text:
CWE-555 J2EE Misconfiguration: Plaintext Password in Configuration File
The J2EE application stores a plaintext password in a configuration file.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Plaintext Password in Configuration File

title: CWE-556: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Use of Identity Impersonation
text:
CWE-556 ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Use of Identity Impersonation
Configuring an ASP.NET application to run with impersonated credentials may give the application unnecessary privileges.

metadata.cwe_name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Use of Identity Impersonation

text:
CWE-558 Use of getlogin() in Multithreaded Application
The product uses the getlogin() function in a multithreaded context, potentially causing it to return incorrect values.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of getlogin() in Multithreaded Application

title: CWE-56: Path Equivalence: 'filedir*' (Wildcard)
text:
CWE-56 Path Equivalence: 'filedir*' (Wildcard)
The product accepts path input in the form of asterisk wildcard ('filedir*') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'filedir*' (Wildcard)

title: CWE-560: Use of umask() with chmod-style Argument
text:
CWE-560 Use of umask() with chmod-style Argument
The product calls umask() with an incorrect argument that is specified as if it is an argument to chmod().

metadata.cwe_name: Use of umask() with chmod-style Argument

title: CWE-561: Dead Code
text:
CWE-561 Dead Code
The product contains dead code, which can never be executed.

metadata.cwe_name: Dead Code

title: CWE-562: Return of Stack Variable Address
text:
CWE-562 Return of Stack Variable Address
A function returns the address of a stack variable, which will cause unintended program behavior, typically in the form of a crash.

metadata.cwe_name: Return of Stack Variable Address

title: CWE-563: Assignment to Variable without Use
text:
CWE-563 Assignment to Variable without Use
The variable's value is assigned but never used, making it a dead store.

metadata.cwe_name: Assignment to Variable without Use

text:
CWE-564 SQL Injection: Hibernate
Using Hibernate to execute a dynamic SQL statement built with user-controlled input can allow an attacker to modify the statement's meaning or to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

metadata.cwe_name: SQL Injection: Hibernate

text:
CWE-565 Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking
The product relies on the existence or values of cookies when performing security-critical operations, but it does not properly ensure that the setting is valid for the associated user.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking

text:
CWE-566 Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled SQL Primary Key
The product uses a database table that includes records that should not be accessible to an actor, but it executes a SQL statement with a primary key that can be controlled by that actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled SQL Primary Key

title: CWE-567: Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in a Multithreaded Context
text:
CWE-567 Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in a Multithreaded Context
The product does not properly synchronize shared data, such as static variables across threads, which can lead to undefined behavior and unpredictable data changes.

metadata.cwe_name: Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in a Multithreaded Context

title: CWE-568: finalize() Method Without super.finalize()
text:
CWE-568 finalize() Method Without super.finalize()
The product contains a finalize() method that does not call super.finalize().

metadata.cwe_name: finalize() Method Without super.finalize()

text:
CWE-57 Path Equivalence: 'fakedir/../realdir/filename'
The product contains protection mechanisms to restrict access to 'realdir/filename', but it constructs pathnames using external input in the form of 'fakedir/../realdir/filename' that are not handled by those mechanisms. This allows attackers to perform unauthorized actions against the targeted file.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: 'fakedir/../realdir/filename'

text:

metadata.cwe_name: Expression is Always False

title: CWE-571: Expression is Always True
text:
CWE-571 Expression is Always True
The product contains an expression that will always evaluate to true.

metadata.cwe_name: Expression is Always True

text:
CWE-572 Call to Thread run() instead of start()

metadata.cwe_name: Call to Thread run() instead of start()

title: CWE-573: Improper Following of Specification by Caller
text:
CWE-573 Improper Following of Specification by Caller
The product does not follow or incorrectly follows the specifications as required by the implementation language, environment, framework, protocol, or platform.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Following of Specification by Caller

CWE-574 EJB Bad Practices: Use of Synchronization Primitives

metadata.cwe_name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Synchronization Primitives

CWE-575 EJB Bad Practices: Use of AWT Swing

metadata.cwe_name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of AWT Swing

title: CWE-576: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Java I/O

CWE-576 EJB Bad Practices: Use of Java I/O

metadata.cwe_name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Java I/O

title: CWE-577: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Sockets
text:
CWE-577 EJB Bad Practices: Use of Sockets
The product violates the Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB) specification by using sockets.

metadata.cwe_name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Sockets

text:
CWE-578 EJB Bad Practices: Use of Class Loader

metadata.cwe_name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Class Loader

text:
CWE-579 J2EE Bad Practices: Non-serializable Object Stored in Session
The product stores a non-serializable object as an HttpSession attribute, which can hurt reliability.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Bad Practices: Non-serializable Object Stored in Session

title: CWE-58: Path Equivalence: Windows 8.3 Filename
text:
CWE-58 Path Equivalence: Windows 8.3 Filename
The product contains a protection mechanism that restricts access to a long filename on a Windows operating system, but it does not properly restrict access to the equivalent short "8.3" filename.

metadata.cwe_name: Path Equivalence: Windows 8.3 Filename

title: CWE-580: clone() Method Without super.clone()
text:
CWE-580 clone() Method Without super.clone()
The product contains a clone() method that does not call super.clone() to obtain the new object.

metadata.cwe_name: clone() Method Without super.clone()

title: CWE-581: Object Model Violation: Just One of Equals and Hashcode Defined
text:
CWE-581 Object Model Violation: Just One of Equals and Hashcode Defined
The product does not maintain equal hashcodes for equal objects.

metadata.cwe_name: Object Model Violation: Just One of Equals and Hashcode Defined

title: CWE-582: Array Declared Public, Final, and Static
text:
CWE-582 Array Declared Public, Final, and Static
The product declares an array public, final, and static, which is not sufficient to prevent the array's contents from being modified.

metadata.cwe_name: Array Declared Public, Final, and Static

title: CWE-583: finalize() Method Declared Public
text:
CWE-583 finalize() Method Declared Public
The product violates secure coding principles for mobile code by declaring a finalize() method public.

metadata.cwe_name: finalize() Method Declared Public

title: CWE-584: Return Inside Finally Block
text:
CWE-584 Return Inside Finally Block
The code has a return statement inside a finally block, which will cause any thrown exception in the try block to be discarded.

metadata.cwe_name: Return Inside Finally Block

title: CWE-585: Empty Synchronized Block
text:
CWE-585 Empty Synchronized Block
The product contains an empty synchronized block.

metadata.cwe_name: Empty Synchronized Block

title: CWE-586: Explicit Call to Finalize()
text:
CWE-586 Explicit Call to Finalize()
The product makes an explicit call to the finalize() method from outside the finalizer.

metadata.cwe_name: Explicit Call to Finalize()

title: CWE-587: Assignment of a Fixed Address to a Pointer
text:
CWE-587 Assignment of a Fixed Address to a Pointer
The product sets a pointer to a specific address other than NULL or 0.

metadata.cwe_name: Assignment of a Fixed Address to a Pointer

title: CWE-588: Attempt to Access Child of a Non-structure Pointer
text:
CWE-588 Attempt to Access Child of a Non-structure Pointer
Casting a non-structure type to a structure type and accessing a field can lead to memory access errors or data corruption.

metadata.cwe_name: Attempt to Access Child of a Non-structure Pointer

title: CWE-589: Call to Non-ubiquitous API
text:
CWE-589 Call to Non-ubiquitous API
The product uses an API function that does not exist on all versions of the target platform. This could cause portability problems or inconsistencies that allow denial of service or other consequences.

metadata.cwe_name: Call to Non-ubiquitous API

title: CWE-59: Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')
text:
CWE-59 Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')
The product attempts to access a file based on the filename, but it does not properly prevent that filename from identifying a link or shortcut that resolves to an unintended resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')

text:
CWE-590 Free of Memory not on the Heap
The product calls free() on a pointer to memory that was not allocated using associated heap allocation functions such as malloc(), calloc(), or realloc().

metadata.cwe_name: Free of Memory not on the Heap

text:
CWE-591 Sensitive Data Storage in Improperly Locked Memory
The product stores sensitive data in memory that is not locked, or that has been incorrectly locked, which might cause the memory to be written to swap files on disk by the virtual memory manager. This can make the data more accessible to external actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Sensitive Data Storage in Improperly Locked Memory

title: CWE-592: DEPRECATED: Authentication Bypass Issues
text:
CWE-592 DEPRECATED: Authentication Bypass Issues
This weakness has been deprecated because it covered redundant concepts already described in CWE-287.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Authentication Bypass Issues

title: CWE-593: Authentication Bypass: OpenSSL CTX Object Modified after SSL Objects are Created
text:
CWE-593 Authentication Bypass: OpenSSL CTX Object Modified after SSL Objects are Created
The product modifies the SSL context after connection creation has begun.

metadata.cwe_name: Authentication Bypass: OpenSSL CTX Object Modified after SSL Objects are Created

title: CWE-594: J2EE Framework: Saving Unserializable Objects to Disk
text:
CWE-594 J2EE Framework: Saving Unserializable Objects to Disk
When the J2EE container attempts to write unserializable objects to disk there is no guarantee that the process will complete successfully.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Framework: Saving Unserializable Objects to Disk

title: CWE-595: Comparison of Object References Instead of Object Contents
text:
CWE-595 Comparison of Object References Instead of Object Contents
The product compares object references instead of the contents of the objects themselves, preventing it from detecting equivalent objects.

metadata.cwe_name: Comparison of Object References Instead of Object Contents

title: CWE-596: DEPRECATED: Incorrect Semantic Object Comparison
text:
CWE-596 DEPRECATED: Incorrect Semantic Object Comparison
This weakness has been deprecated. It was poorly described and difficult to distinguish from other entries. It was also inappropriate to assign a separate ID solely because of domain-specific considerations. Its closest equivalent is CWE-1023.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Incorrect Semantic Object Comparison

title: CWE-597: Use of Wrong Operator in String Comparison
text:
CWE-597 Use of Wrong Operator in String Comparison
The product uses the wrong operator when comparing a string, such as using "==" when the .equals() method should be used instead.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Wrong Operator in String Comparison

title: CWE-598: Use of HTTP Request With Sensitive Query String
text:
CWE-598 Use of HTTP Request With Sensitive Query String
The web application uses an HTTP method to process a request, but the request includes sensitive information in the query string.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of HTTP Request With Sensitive Query String

title: CWE-599: Missing Validation of OpenSSL Certificate
text:
CWE-599 Missing Validation of OpenSSL Certificate

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Validation of OpenSSL Certificate

title: CWE-6: J2EE Misconfiguration: Insufficient Session-ID Length
text:
CWE-6 J2EE Misconfiguration: Insufficient Session-ID Length
The J2EE application is configured to use an insufficient session ID length.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Insufficient Session-ID Length

title: CWE-600: Uncaught Exception in Servlet
text:
CWE-600 Uncaught Exception in Servlet
The Servlet does not catch all exceptions, which may reveal sensitive debugging information.

metadata.cwe_name: Uncaught Exception in Servlet

title: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
text:
CWE-601 URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

metadata.cwe_name: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

title: CWE-602: Client-Side Enforcement of Server-Side Security
text:
CWE-602 Client-Side Enforcement of Server-Side Security
The product is composed of a server that relies on the client to implement a mechanism that is intended to protect the server.

metadata.cwe_name: Client-Side Enforcement of Server-Side Security

text:
CWE-603 Use of Client-Side Authentication

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Client-Side Authentication

text:
CWE-605 Multiple Binds to the Same Port

metadata.cwe_name: Multiple Binds to the Same Port

title: CWE-606: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
text:
CWE-606 Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
The product does not properly check inputs that are used for loop conditions, potentially leading to a denial of service or other consequences because of excessive looping.

metadata.cwe_name: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition

title: CWE-607: Public Static Final Field References Mutable Object
text:
CWE-607 Public Static Final Field References Mutable Object
A public or protected static final field references a mutable object, which allows the object to be changed by malicious code, or accidentally from another package.

metadata.cwe_name: Public Static Final Field References Mutable Object

title: CWE-608: Struts: Non-private Field in ActionForm Class
text:
CWE-608 Struts: Non-private Field in ActionForm Class
An ActionForm class contains a field that has not been declared private, which can be accessed without using a setter or getter.

metadata.cwe_name: Struts: Non-private Field in ActionForm Class

title: CWE-609: Double-Checked Locking
text:
CWE-609 Double-Checked Locking
The product uses double-checked locking to access a resource without the overhead of explicit synchronization, but the locking is insufficient.

metadata.cwe_name: Double-Checked Locking

title: CWE-61: UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following
text:
CWE-61 UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following
The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently account for when the file is a symbolic link that resolves to a target outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

metadata.cwe_name: UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following

title: CWE-610: Externally Controlled Reference to a Resource in Another Sphere
text:
CWE-610 Externally Controlled Reference to a Resource in Another Sphere
The product uses an externally controlled name or reference that resolves to a resource that is outside of the intended control sphere.

metadata.cwe_name: Externally Controlled Reference to a Resource in Another Sphere

title: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
text:
CWE-611 Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
The product processes an XML document that can contain XML entities with URIs that resolve to documents outside of the intended sphere of control, causing the product to embed incorrect documents into its output.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

title: CWE-612: Improper Authorization of Index Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-612 Improper Authorization of Index Containing Sensitive Information
The product creates a search index of private or sensitive documents, but it does not properly limit index access to actors who are authorized to see the original information.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Authorization of Index Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-613: Insufficient Session Expiration
text:
CWE-613 Insufficient Session Expiration
According to WASC, "Insufficient Session Expiration is when a web site permits an attacker to reuse old session credentials or session IDs for authorization."

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Session Expiration

title: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute
text:
CWE-614 Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute
The Secure attribute for sensitive cookies in HTTPS sessions is not set.

metadata.cwe_name: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute

title: CWE-615: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code Comments
text:
CWE-615 Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code Comments
While adding general comments is very useful, some programmers tend to leave important data, such as: filenames related to the web application, old links or links which were not meant to be browsed by users, old code fragments, etc.

metadata.cwe_name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code Comments

title: CWE-616: Incomplete Identification of Uploaded File Variables (PHP)
text:
CWE-616 Incomplete Identification of Uploaded File Variables (PHP)
The PHP application uses an old method for processing uploaded files by referencing the four global variables that are set for each file (e.g. $varname, $varname_size, $varname_name, $varname_type). These variables could be overwritten by attackers, causing the application to process unauthorized files.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Identification of Uploaded File Variables (PHP)

title: CWE-617: Reachable Assertion
text:
CWE-617 Reachable Assertion
The product contains an assert() or similar statement that can be triggered by an attacker, which leads to an application exit or other behavior that is more severe than necessary.

metadata.cwe_name: Reachable Assertion

title: CWE-618: Exposed Unsafe ActiveX Method
text:
CWE-618 Exposed Unsafe ActiveX Method
An ActiveX control is intended for use in a web browser, but it exposes dangerous methods that perform actions that are outside of the browser's security model (e.g. the zone or domain).

metadata.cwe_name: Exposed Unsafe ActiveX Method

title: CWE-619: Dangling Database Cursor ('Cursor Injection')
text:
CWE-619 Dangling Database Cursor ('Cursor Injection')
If a database cursor is not closed properly, then it could become accessible to other users while retaining the same privileges that were originally assigned, leaving the cursor "dangling."

metadata.cwe_name: Dangling Database Cursor ('Cursor Injection')

title: CWE-62: UNIX Hard Link
text:
CWE-62 UNIX Hard Link
The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently account for when the name is associated with a hard link to a target that is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

metadata.cwe_name: UNIX Hard Link

title: CWE-620: Unverified Password Change
text:
CWE-620 Unverified Password Change
When setting a new password for a user, the product does not require knowledge of the original password, or using another form of authentication.

metadata.cwe_name: Unverified Password Change

title: CWE-621: Variable Extraction Error
text:
CWE-621 Variable Extraction Error
The product uses external input to determine the names of variables into which information is extracted, without verifying that the names of the specified variables are valid. This could cause the program to overwrite unintended variables.

metadata.cwe_name: Variable Extraction Error

title: CWE-622: Improper Validation of Function Hook Arguments
text:
CWE-622 Improper Validation of Function Hook Arguments
The product adds hooks to user-accessible API functions, but it does not properly validate the arguments. This could lead to resultant vulnerabilities.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Validation of Function Hook Arguments

title: CWE-623: Unsafe ActiveX Control Marked Safe For Scripting
text:
CWE-623 Unsafe ActiveX Control Marked Safe For Scripting
An ActiveX control is intended for restricted use, but it has been marked as safe-for-scripting.

metadata.cwe_name: Unsafe ActiveX Control Marked Safe For Scripting

title: CWE-624: Executable Regular Expression Error
text:
CWE-624 Executable Regular Expression Error
The product uses a regular expression that either (1) contains an executable component with user-controlled inputs, or (2) allows a user to enable execution by inserting pattern modifiers.

metadata.cwe_name: Executable Regular Expression Error

title: CWE-625: Permissive Regular Expression
text:
CWE-625 Permissive Regular Expression
The product uses a regular expression that does not sufficiently restrict the set of allowed values.

metadata.cwe_name: Permissive Regular Expression

title: CWE-626: Null Byte Interaction Error (Poison Null Byte)
text:
CWE-626 Null Byte Interaction Error (Poison Null Byte)
The product does not properly handle null bytes or NUL characters when passing data between different representations or components.

metadata.cwe_name: Null Byte Interaction Error (Poison Null Byte)

title: CWE-627: Dynamic Variable Evaluation
text:
CWE-627 Dynamic Variable Evaluation
In a language where the user can influence the name of a variable at runtime, if the variable names are not controlled, an attacker can read or write to arbitrary variables, or access arbitrary functions.

metadata.cwe_name: Dynamic Variable Evaluation

title: CWE-628: Function Call with Incorrectly Specified Arguments
text:
CWE-628 Function Call with Incorrectly Specified Arguments
The product calls a function, procedure, or routine with arguments that are not correctly specified, leading to always-incorrect behavior and resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Function Call with Incorrectly Specified Arguments

title: CWE-636: Not Failing Securely ('Failing Open')
text:
CWE-636 Not Failing Securely ('Failing Open')
When the product encounters an error condition or failure, its design requires it to fall back to a state that is less secure than other options that are available, such as selecting the weakest encryption algorithm or using the most permissive access control restrictions.

metadata.cwe_name: Not Failing Securely ('Failing Open')

title: CWE-637: Unnecessary Complexity in Protection Mechanism (Not Using 'Economy of Mechanism')
text:
CWE-637 Unnecessary Complexity in Protection Mechanism (Not Using 'Economy of Mechanism')
The product uses a more complex mechanism than necessary, which could lead to resultant weaknesses when the mechanism is not correctly understood, modeled, configured, implemented, or used.

metadata.cwe_name: Unnecessary Complexity in Protection Mechanism (Not Using 'Economy of Mechanism')

title: CWE-638: Not Using Complete Mediation
text:
CWE-638 Not Using Complete Mediation
The product does not perform access checks on a resource every time the resource is accessed by an entity, which can create resultant weaknesses if that entity's rights or privileges change over time.

metadata.cwe_name: Not Using Complete Mediation

title: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
text:
CWE-639 Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
The system's authorization functionality does not prevent one user from gaining access to another user's data or record by modifying the key value identifying the data.

metadata.cwe_name: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

title: CWE-64: Windows Shortcut Following (.LNK)
text:
CWE-64 Windows Shortcut Following (.LNK)
The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently handle when the file is a Windows shortcut (.LNK) whose target is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

metadata.cwe_name: Windows Shortcut Following (.LNK)

title: CWE-640: Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password
text:
CWE-640 Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password
The product contains a mechanism for users to recover or change their passwords without knowing the original password, but the mechanism is weak.

metadata.cwe_name: Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password

title: CWE-641: Improper Restriction of Names for Files and Other Resources
text:
CWE-641 Improper Restriction of Names for Files and Other Resources
The product constructs the name of a file or other resource using input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the resulting name.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Names for Files and Other Resources

title: CWE-642: External Control of Critical State Data
text:
CWE-642 External Control of Critical State Data
The product stores security-critical state information about its users, or the product itself, in a location that is accessible to unauthorized actors.

metadata.cwe_name: External Control of Critical State Data

title: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')
text:
CWE-643 Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')
The product uses external input to dynamically construct an XPath expression used to retrieve data from an XML database, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes that input. This allows an attacker to control the structure of the query.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')

title: CWE-644: Improper Neutralization of HTTP Headers for Scripting Syntax
text:
CWE-644 Improper Neutralization of HTTP Headers for Scripting Syntax
The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes web scripting syntax in HTTP headers that can be used by web browser components that can process raw headers, such as Flash.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of HTTP Headers for Scripting Syntax

title: CWE-645: Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism
text:
CWE-645 Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism
The product contains an account lockout protection mechanism, but the mechanism is too restrictive and can be triggered too easily, which allows attackers to deny service to legitimate users by causing their accounts to be locked out.

metadata.cwe_name: Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism

title: CWE-646: Reliance on File Name or Extension of Externally-Supplied File
text:
CWE-646 Reliance on File Name or Extension of Externally-Supplied File
The product allows a file to be uploaded, but it relies on the file name or extension of the file to determine the appropriate behaviors. This could be used by attackers to cause the file to be misclassified and processed in a dangerous fashion.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on File Name or Extension of Externally-Supplied File

title: CWE-647: Use of Non-Canonical URL Paths for Authorization Decisions
text:
CWE-647 Use of Non-Canonical URL Paths for Authorization Decisions
The product defines policy namespaces and makes authorization decisions based on the assumption that a URL is canonical. This can allow a non-canonical URL to bypass the authorization.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Non-Canonical URL Paths for Authorization Decisions

title: CWE-648: Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs
text:
CWE-648 Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs
The product does not conform to the API requirements for a function call that requires extra privileges. This could allow attackers to gain privileges by causing the function to be called incorrectly.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs

title: CWE-649: Reliance on Obfuscation or Encryption of Security-Relevant Inputs without Integrity Checking
text:
CWE-649 Reliance on Obfuscation or Encryption of Security-Relevant Inputs without Integrity Checking
The product uses obfuscation or encryption of inputs that should not be mutable by an external actor, but the product does not use integrity checks to detect if those inputs have been modified.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Obfuscation or Encryption of Security-Relevant Inputs without Integrity Checking

title: CWE-65: Windows Hard Link
text:
CWE-65 Windows Hard Link
The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently handle when the name is associated with a hard link to a target that is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

metadata.cwe_name: Windows Hard Link

title: CWE-650: Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side
text:
CWE-650 Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side
The server contains a protection mechanism that assumes that any URI that is accessed using HTTP GET will not cause a state change to the associated resource. This might allow attackers to bypass intended access restrictions and conduct resource modification and deletion attacks, since some applications allow GET to modify state.

metadata.cwe_name: Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side

title: CWE-651: Exposure of WSDL File Containing Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-651 Exposure of WSDL File Containing Sensitive Information
The Web services architecture may require exposing a Web Service Definition Language (WSDL) file that contains information on the publicly accessible services and how callers of these services should interact with them (e.g. what parameters they expect and what types they return).

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of WSDL File Containing Sensitive Information

title: CWE-652: Improper Neutralization of Data within XQuery Expressions ('XQuery Injection')
text:
CWE-652 Improper Neutralization of Data within XQuery Expressions ('XQuery Injection')
The product uses external input to dynamically construct an XQuery expression used to retrieve data from an XML database, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes that input. This allows an attacker to control the structure of the query.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Data within XQuery Expressions ('XQuery Injection')

title: CWE-653: Improper Isolation or Compartmentalization
text:
CWE-653 Improper Isolation or Compartmentalization
The product does not properly compartmentalize or isolate functionality, processes, or resources that require different privilege levels, rights, or permissions.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Isolation or Compartmentalization

title: CWE-654: Reliance on a Single Factor in a Security Decision
text:
CWE-654 Reliance on a Single Factor in a Security Decision
A protection mechanism relies exclusively, or to a large extent, on the evaluation of a single condition or the integrity of a single object or entity in order to make a decision about granting access to restricted resources or functionality.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on a Single Factor in a Security Decision

title: CWE-655: Insufficient Psychological Acceptability
text:
CWE-655 Insufficient Psychological Acceptability
The product has a protection mechanism that is too difficult or inconvenient to use, encouraging non-malicious users to disable or bypass the mechanism, whether by accident or on purpose.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Psychological Acceptability

title: CWE-656: Reliance on Security Through Obscurity
text:
CWE-656 Reliance on Security Through Obscurity
The product uses a protection mechanism whose strength depends heavily on its obscurity, such that knowledge of its algorithms or key data is sufficient to defeat the mechanism.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Security Through Obscurity

title: CWE-657: Violation of Secure Design Principles
text:
CWE-657 Violation of Secure Design Principles
The product violates well-established principles for secure design.

metadata.cwe_name: Violation of Secure Design Principles

title: CWE-66: Improper Handling of File Names that Identify Virtual Resources
text:
CWE-66 Improper Handling of File Names that Identify Virtual Resources
The product does not handle or incorrectly handles a file name that identifies a "virtual" resource that is not directly specified within the directory that is associated with the file name, causing the product to perform file-based operations on a resource that is not a file.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of File Names that Identify Virtual Resources

title: CWE-662: Improper Synchronization
text:
CWE-662 Improper Synchronization
The product utilizes multiple threads, processes, components, or systems to allow temporary access to a shared resource that can only be exclusive to one process at a time, but it does not properly synchronize these actions, which might cause simultaneous accesses of this resource by multiple threads or processes.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Synchronization

title: CWE-663: Use of a Non-reentrant Function in a Concurrent Context
text:
CWE-663 Use of a Non-reentrant Function in a Concurrent Context
The product calls a non-reentrant function in a concurrent context in which a competing code sequence (e.g. thread or signal handler) may have an opportunity to call the same function or otherwise influence its state.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of a Non-reentrant Function in a Concurrent Context

title: CWE-664: Improper Control of a Resource Through its Lifetime
text:
CWE-664 Improper Control of a Resource Through its Lifetime
The product does not maintain or incorrectly maintains control over a resource throughout its lifetime of creation, use, and release.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of a Resource Through its Lifetime

title: CWE-665: Improper Initialization
text:
CWE-665 Improper Initialization
The product does not initialize or incorrectly initializes a resource, which might leave the resource in an unexpected state when it is accessed or used.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Initialization

title: CWE-666: Operation on Resource in Wrong Phase of Lifetime
text:
CWE-666 Operation on Resource in Wrong Phase of Lifetime
The product performs an operation on a resource at the wrong phase of the resource's lifecycle, which can lead to unexpected behaviors.

metadata.cwe_name: Operation on Resource in Wrong Phase of Lifetime

title: CWE-667: Improper Locking
text:
CWE-667 Improper Locking
The product does not properly acquire or release a lock on a resource, leading to unexpected resource state changes and behaviors.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Locking

title: CWE-668: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere
text:
CWE-668 Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere
The product exposes a resource to the wrong control sphere, providing unintended actors with inappropriate access to the resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere

title: CWE-669: Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres
text:
CWE-669 Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres
The product does not properly transfer a resource/behavior to another sphere, or improperly imports a resource/behavior from another sphere, in a manner that provides unintended control over that resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres

title: CWE-67: Improper Handling of Windows Device Names
text:
CWE-67 Improper Handling of Windows Device Names
The product constructs pathnames from user input, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles a pathname containing a Windows device name such as AUX or CON. This typically leads to denial of service or an information exposure when the application attempts to process the pathname as a regular file.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Windows Device Names

title: CWE-670: Always-Incorrect Control Flow Implementation
text:
CWE-670 Always-Incorrect Control Flow Implementation
The code contains a control flow path that does not reflect the algorithm that the path is intended to implement, leading to incorrect behavior any time this path is navigated.

metadata.cwe_name: Always-Incorrect Control Flow Implementation

title: CWE-671: Lack of Administrator Control over Security
text:
CWE-671 Lack of Administrator Control over Security
The product uses security features in a way that prevents the product's administrator from tailoring security settings to reflect the environment in which the product is being used. This introduces resultant weaknesses or prevents it from operating at a level of security that is desired by the administrator.

metadata.cwe_name: Lack of Administrator Control over Security

title: CWE-672: Operation on a Resource after Expiration or Release
text:
CWE-672 Operation on a Resource after Expiration or Release
The product uses, accesses, or otherwise operates on a resource after that resource has been expired, released, or revoked.

metadata.cwe_name: Operation on a Resource after Expiration or Release

title: CWE-673: External Influence of Sphere Definition
text:
CWE-673 External Influence of Sphere Definition
The product does not prevent the definition of control spheres from external actors.

metadata.cwe_name: External Influence of Sphere Definition

title: CWE-674: Uncontrolled Recursion
text:
CWE-674 Uncontrolled Recursion
The product does not properly control the amount of recursion that takes place, consuming excessive resources, such as allocated memory or the program stack.

metadata.cwe_name: Uncontrolled Recursion

title: CWE-675: Multiple Operations on Resource in Single-Operation Context
text:
CWE-675 Multiple Operations on Resource in Single-Operation Context
The product performs the same operation on a resource two or more times, when the operation should only be applied once.

metadata.cwe_name: Multiple Operations on Resource in Single-Operation Context

title: CWE-676: Use of Potentially Dangerous Function
text:
CWE-676 Use of Potentially Dangerous Function
The product invokes a potentially dangerous function that could introduce a vulnerability if it is used incorrectly, but the function can also be used safely.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Potentially Dangerous Function

title: CWE-680: Integer Overflow to Buffer Overflow
text:
CWE-680 Integer Overflow to Buffer Overflow
The product performs a calculation to determine how much memory to allocate, but an integer overflow can occur that causes less memory to be allocated than expected, leading to a buffer overflow.

metadata.cwe_name: Integer Overflow to Buffer Overflow

title: CWE-681: Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types
text:
CWE-681 Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types
When converting from one data type to another, such as long to integer, data can be omitted or translated in a way that produces unexpected values. If the resulting values are used in a sensitive context, then dangerous behaviors may occur.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types

title: CWE-682: Incorrect Calculation
text:
CWE-682 Incorrect Calculation
The product performs a calculation that generates incorrect or unintended results that are later used in security-critical decisions or resource management.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Calculation

title: CWE-683: Function Call With Incorrect Order of Arguments
text:
CWE-683 Function Call With Incorrect Order of Arguments
The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies the arguments in an incorrect order, leading to resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Function Call With Incorrect Order of Arguments

title: CWE-684: Incorrect Provision of Specified Functionality
text:
CWE-684 Incorrect Provision of Specified Functionality
The code does not function according to its published specifications, potentially leading to incorrect usage.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Provision of Specified Functionality

title: CWE-685: Function Call With Incorrect Number of Arguments
text:
CWE-685 Function Call With Incorrect Number of Arguments
The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies too many arguments, or too few arguments, which may lead to undefined behavior and resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Function Call With Incorrect Number of Arguments

title: CWE-686: Function Call With Incorrect Argument Type
text:
CWE-686 Function Call With Incorrect Argument Type
The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies an argument that is the wrong data type, which may lead to resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Function Call With Incorrect Argument Type

title: CWE-687: Function Call With Incorrectly Specified Argument Value
text:
CWE-687 Function Call With Incorrectly Specified Argument Value
The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies an argument that contains the wrong value, which may lead to resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Function Call With Incorrectly Specified Argument Value

title: CWE-688: Function Call With Incorrect Variable or Reference as Argument
text:
CWE-688 Function Call With Incorrect Variable or Reference as Argument
The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies the wrong variable or reference as one of the arguments, which may lead to undefined behavior and resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Function Call With Incorrect Variable or Reference as Argument

title: CWE-689: Permission Race Condition During Resource Copy
text:
CWE-689 Permission Race Condition During Resource Copy
The product, while copying or cloning a resource, does not set the resource's permissions or access control until the copy is complete, leaving the resource exposed to other spheres while the copy is taking place.

metadata.cwe_name: Permission Race Condition During Resource Copy

title: CWE-69: Improper Handling of Windows ::DATA Alternate Data Stream
text:
CWE-69 Improper Handling of Windows ::DATA Alternate Data Stream
The product does not properly prevent access to, or detect usage of, alternate data streams (ADS).

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Windows ::DATA Alternate Data Stream

title: CWE-690: Unchecked Return Value to NULL Pointer Dereference
text:
CWE-690 Unchecked Return Value to NULL Pointer Dereference
The product does not check for an error after calling a function that can return with a NULL pointer if the function fails, which leads to a resultant NULL pointer dereference.

metadata.cwe_name: Unchecked Return Value to NULL Pointer Dereference

title: CWE-691: Insufficient Control Flow Management
text:
CWE-691 Insufficient Control Flow Management
The code does not sufficiently manage its control flow during execution, creating conditions in which the control flow can be modified in unexpected ways.

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Control Flow Management

title: CWE-692: Incomplete Denylist to Cross-Site Scripting
text:
CWE-692 Incomplete Denylist to Cross-Site Scripting
The product uses a denylist-based protection mechanism to defend against XSS attacks, but the denylist is incomplete, allowing XSS variants to succeed.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Denylist to Cross-Site Scripting

title: CWE-693: Protection Mechanism Failure
text:
CWE-693 Protection Mechanism Failure
The product does not use or incorrectly uses a protection mechanism that provides sufficient defense against directed attacks against the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Protection Mechanism Failure

title: CWE-694: Use of Multiple Resources with Duplicate Identifier
text:
CWE-694 Use of Multiple Resources with Duplicate Identifier
The product uses multiple resources that can have the same identifier, in a context in which unique identifiers are required.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Multiple Resources with Duplicate Identifier

title: CWE-695: Use of Low-Level Functionality
text:
CWE-695 Use of Low-Level Functionality
The product uses low-level functionality that is explicitly prohibited by the framework or specification under which the product is supposed to operate.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Low-Level Functionality

title: CWE-696: Incorrect Behavior Order
text:
CWE-696 Incorrect Behavior Order
The product performs multiple related behaviors, but the behaviors are performed in the wrong order in ways that may produce resultant weaknesses.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Behavior Order

title: CWE-697: Incorrect Comparison
text:
CWE-697 Incorrect Comparison
The product compares two entities in a security-relevant context, but the comparison is incorrect.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Comparison

title: CWE-698: Execution After Redirect (EAR)
text:
CWE-698 Execution After Redirect (EAR)
The web application sends a redirect to another location, but instead of exiting, it executes additional code.

metadata.cwe_name: Execution After Redirect (EAR)

title: CWE-7: J2EE Misconfiguration: Missing Custom Error Page
text:
CWE-7 J2EE Misconfiguration: Missing Custom Error Page
The default error page of a web application should not display sensitive information about the product.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Missing Custom Error Page

title: CWE-703: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions
text:
CWE-703 Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions
The product does not properly anticipate or handle exceptional conditions that rarely occur during normal operation of the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions

title: CWE-704: Incorrect Type Conversion or Cast
text:
CWE-704 Incorrect Type Conversion or Cast
The product does not correctly convert an object, resource, or structure from one type to a different type.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Type Conversion or Cast

title: CWE-705: Incorrect Control Flow Scoping
text:
CWE-705 Incorrect Control Flow Scoping
The product does not properly return control flow to the proper location after it has completed a task or detected an unusual condition.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Control Flow Scoping

title: CWE-706: Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference
text:
CWE-706 Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference
The product uses a name or reference to access a resource, but the name/reference resolves to a resource that is outside of the intended control sphere.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference

title: CWE-707: Improper Neutralization
text:
CWE-707 Improper Neutralization
The product does not ensure or incorrectly ensures that structured messages or data are well-formed and that certain security properties are met before being read from an upstream component or sent to a downstream component.

title: CWE-708: Incorrect Ownership Assignment

title: CWE-710: Improper Adherence to Coding Standards

title: CWE-72: Improper Handling of Apple HFS+ Alternate Data Stream Path

title: CWE-732: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

title: CWE-733: Compiler Optimization Removal or Modification of Security-critical Code

title: CWE-74: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component ('Injection')

The product constructs all or part of a command, data structure, or record using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify how it is parsed or interpreted when it is sent to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component ('Injection')

title: CWE-749: Exposed Dangerous Method or Function
text:
CWE-749 Exposed Dangerous Method or Function

metadata.cwe_name: Exposed Dangerous Method or Function

title: CWE-75: Failure to Sanitize Special Elements into a Different Plane (Special Element Injection)
text:
CWE-75 Failure to Sanitize Special Elements into a Different Plane (Special Element Injection)
The product does not adequately filter user-controlled input for special elements with control implications.

metadata.cwe_name: Failure to Sanitize Special Elements into a Different Plane (Special Element Injection)

title: CWE-754: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
text:
CWE-754 Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
The product does not check or incorrectly checks for unusual or exceptional conditions that are not expected to occur frequently during day to day operation of the product.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions

title: CWE-755: Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions
text:
CWE-755 Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions
The product does not handle or incorrectly handles an exceptional condition.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions

title: CWE-756: Missing Custom Error Page
text:
CWE-756 Missing Custom Error Page
The product does not return custom error pages to the user, possibly exposing sensitive information.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Custom Error Page

title: CWE-757: Selection of Less-Secure Algorithm During Negotiation ('Algorithm Downgrade')
text:
CWE-757 Selection of Less-Secure Algorithm During Negotiation ('Algorithm Downgrade')
A protocol or its implementation supports interaction between multiple actors and allows those actors to negotiate which algorithm should be used as a protection mechanism such as encryption or authentication, but it does not select the strongest algorithm that is available to both parties.

metadata.cwe_name: Selection of Less-Secure Algorithm During Negotiation ('Algorithm Downgrade')

title: CWE-758: Reliance on Undefined, Unspecified, or Implementation-Defined Behavior
text:
CWE-758 Reliance on Undefined, Unspecified, or Implementation-Defined Behavior
The product uses an API function, data structure, or other entity in a way that relies on properties that are not always guaranteed to hold for that entity.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Undefined, Unspecified, or Implementation-Defined Behavior

title: CWE-759: Use of a One-Way Hash without a Salt
text:
CWE-759 Use of a One-Way Hash without a Salt
The product uses a one-way cryptographic hash against an input that should not be reversible, such as a password, but the product does not also use a salt as part of the input.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of a One-Way Hash without a Salt

title: CWE-76: Improper Neutralization of Equivalent Special Elements
text:
CWE-76 Improper Neutralization of Equivalent Special Elements
The product correctly neutralizes certain special elements, but it improperly neutralizes equivalent special elements.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Equivalent Special Elements

title: CWE-760: Use of a One-Way Hash with a Predictable Salt
text:
CWE-760 Use of a One-Way Hash with a Predictable Salt
The product uses a one-way cryptographic hash against an input that should not be reversible, such as a password, but the product uses a predictable salt as part of the input.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of a One-Way Hash with a Predictable Salt

title: CWE-761: Free of Pointer not at Start of Buffer
text:
CWE-761 Free of Pointer not at Start of Buffer
The product calls free() on a pointer to a memory resource that was allocated on the heap, but the pointer is not at the start of the buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Free of Pointer not at Start of Buffer

title: CWE-762: Mismatched Memory Management Routines
text:
CWE-762 Mismatched Memory Management Routines
The product attempts to return a memory resource to the system, but it calls a release function that is not compatible with the function that was originally used to allocate that resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Mismatched Memory Management Routines

title: CWE-763: Release of Invalid Pointer or Reference
text:
CWE-763 Release of Invalid Pointer or Reference
The product attempts to return a memory resource to the system, but it calls the wrong release function or calls the appropriate release function incorrectly.

metadata.cwe_name: Release of Invalid Pointer or Reference

title: CWE-764: Multiple Locks of a Critical Resource
text:
CWE-764 Multiple Locks of a Critical Resource
The product locks a critical resource more times than intended, leading to an unexpected state in the system.

metadata.cwe_name: Multiple Locks of a Critical Resource

title: CWE-765: Multiple Unlocks of a Critical Resource
text:
CWE-765 Multiple Unlocks of a Critical Resource
The product unlocks a critical resource more times than intended, leading to an unexpected state in the system.

metadata.cwe_name: Multiple Unlocks of a Critical Resource

title: CWE-766: Critical Data Element Declared Public
text:
CWE-766 Critical Data Element Declared Public
The product declares a critical variable, field, or member to be public when intended security policy requires it to be private.

metadata.cwe_name: Critical Data Element Declared Public

title: CWE-767: Access to Critical Private Variable via Public Method
text:
CWE-767 Access to Critical Private Variable via Public Method
The product defines a public method that reads or modifies a private variable.

metadata.cwe_name: Access to Critical Private Variable via Public Method

title: CWE-768: Incorrect Short Circuit Evaluation
text:
CWE-768 Incorrect Short Circuit Evaluation
The product contains a conditional statement with multiple logical expressions in which one of the non-leading expressions may produce side effects. This may lead to an unexpected state in the program after the execution of the conditional, because short-circuiting logic may prevent the side effects from occurring.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Short Circuit Evaluation

title: CWE-769: DEPRECATED: Uncontrolled File Descriptor Consumption
text:

title: CWE-77: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')
text:
CWE-77 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')
The product constructs all or part of a command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended command when it is sent to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')

title: CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
text:
CWE-770 Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
The product allocates a reusable resource or group of resources on behalf of an actor without imposing any intended restrictions on the size or number of resources that can be allocated.

metadata.cwe_name: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

title: CWE-771: Missing Reference to Active Allocated Resource
text:
CWE-771 Missing Reference to Active Allocated Resource
The product does not properly maintain a reference to a resource that has been allocated, which prevents the resource from being reclaimed.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Reference to Active Allocated Resource

title: CWE-772: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime
text:
CWE-772 Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime
The product does not release a resource after its effective lifetime has ended, i.e., after the resource is no longer needed.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime

title: CWE-773: Missing Reference to Active File Descriptor or Handle
text:
CWE-773 Missing Reference to Active File Descriptor or Handle
The product does not properly maintain references to a file descriptor or handle, which prevents that file descriptor/handle from being reclaimed.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Reference to Active File Descriptor or Handle

title: CWE-774: Allocation of File Descriptors or Handles Without Limits or Throttling
text:
CWE-774 Allocation of File Descriptors or Handles Without Limits or Throttling
The product allocates file descriptors or handles on behalf of an actor without imposing any restrictions on how many descriptors can be allocated, in violation of the intended security policy for that actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Allocation of File Descriptors or Handles Without Limits or Throttling

title: CWE-775: Missing Release of File Descriptor or Handle after Effective Lifetime
text:
CWE-775 Missing Release of File Descriptor or Handle after Effective Lifetime
The product does not release a file descriptor or handle after its effective lifetime has ended, i.e., after the file descriptor/handle is no longer needed.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Release of File Descriptor or Handle after Effective Lifetime

title: CWE-776: Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')
text:
CWE-776 Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')
The product uses XML documents and allows their structure to be defined with a Document Type Definition (DTD), but it does not properly control the number of recursive definitions of entities.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')

title: CWE-777: Regular Expression without Anchors
text:
CWE-777 Regular Expression without Anchors

metadata.cwe_name: Regular Expression without Anchors

title: CWE-778: Insufficient Logging
text:
CWE-778 Insufficient Logging

metadata.cwe_name: Insufficient Logging

title: CWE-779: Logging of Excessive Data
text:
CWE-779 Logging of Excessive Data
The product logs too much information, making log files hard to process and possibly hindering recovery efforts or forensic analysis after an attack.

metadata.cwe_name: Logging of Excessive Data

title: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
text:
CWE-78 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
The product constructs all or part of an OS command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended OS command when it is sent to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

metadata.cwe_name: Use of RSA Algorithm without OAEP

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Address Validation in IOCTL with METHOD_NEITHER I/O Control Code

CWE-782 Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control

metadata.cwe_name: Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control

text:

metadata.cwe_name: Operator Precedence Logic Error

text:

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking in a Security Decision

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Path Manipulation Function without Maximum-sized Buffer

metadata.cwe_name: Access of Memory Location Before Start of Buffer

text:
CWE-788 Access of Memory Location After End of Buffer

metadata.cwe_name: Access of Memory Location After End of Buffer

text:
CWE-789 Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value
The product allocates memory based on an untrusted, large size value, but it does not ensure that the size is within expected limits, allowing arbitrary amounts of memory to be allocated.

metadata.cwe_name: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value

The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

title: CWE-790: Improper Filtering of Special Elements
text:
CWE-790 Improper Filtering of Special Elements

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Filtering of Special Elements

title: CWE-791: Incomplete Filtering of Special Elements
text:
CWE-791 Incomplete Filtering of Special Elements
The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not completely filter special elements before sending it to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Filtering of Special Elements

text:
CWE-792 Incomplete Filtering of One or More Instances of Special Elements
The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not completely filter one or more instances of special elements before sending it to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Filtering of One or More Instances of Special Elements

title: CWE-793: Only Filtering One Instance of a Special Element
text:
CWE-793 Only Filtering One Instance of a Special Element
The product receives data from an upstream component, but only filters a single instance of a special element before sending it to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Only Filtering One Instance of a Special Element

text:
CWE-794 Incomplete Filtering of Multiple Instances of Special Elements
The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not filter all instances of a special element before sending it to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Incomplete Filtering of Multiple Instances of Special Elements

text:
CWE-795 Only Filtering Special Elements at a Specified Location
The product receives data from an upstream component, but only accounts for special elements at a specified location, thereby missing remaining special elements that may exist before sending it to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Only Filtering Special Elements at a Specified Location

text:
CWE-796 Only Filtering Special Elements Relative to a Marker
The product receives data from an upstream component, but only accounts for special elements positioned relative to a marker (e.g. "at the beginning/end of a string; the second argument"), thereby missing remaining special elements that may exist before sending it to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Only Filtering Special Elements Relative to a Marker

title: CWE-797: Only Filtering Special Elements at an Absolute Position
text:
CWE-797 Only Filtering Special Elements at an Absolute Position
The product receives data from an upstream component, but only accounts for special elements at an absolute position (e.g. "byte number 10"), thereby missing remaining special elements that may exist before sending it to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Only Filtering Special Elements at an Absolute Position

title: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
text:
CWE-798 Use of Hard-coded Credentials
The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

title: CWE-799: Improper Control of Interaction Frequency
text:
CWE-799 Improper Control of Interaction Frequency
The product does not properly limit the number or frequency of interactions that it has with an actor, such as the number of incoming requests.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of Interaction Frequency

title: CWE-8: J2EE Misconfiguration: Entity Bean Declared Remote
text:

When an application exposes a remote interface for an entity bean, it might also expose methods that get or set the bean's data. These methods could be leveraged to read sensitive information, or to change data in ways that violate the application's expectations, potentially leading to other vulnerabilities.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Entity Bean Declared Remote

title: CWE-80: Improper Neutralization of Script-Related HTML Tags in a Web Page (Basic XSS)
text:
CWE-80 Improper Neutralization of Script-Related HTML Tags in a Web Page (Basic XSS)
