metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Script-Related HTML Tags in a Web Page (Basic XSS)

title: CWE-804: Guessable CAPTCHA
text:
CWE-804 Guessable CAPTCHA
The product uses a CAPTCHA challenge, but the challenge can be guessed or automatically recognized by a non-human actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Guessable CAPTCHA

title: CWE-805: Buffer Access with Incorrect Length Value
text:
CWE-805 Buffer Access with Incorrect Length Value
The product uses a sequential operation to read or write a buffer, but it uses an incorrect length value that causes it to access memory that is outside of the bounds of the buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Buffer Access with Incorrect Length Value

title: CWE-806: Buffer Access Using Size of Source Buffer
text:
CWE-806 Buffer Access Using Size of Source Buffer
The product uses the size of a source buffer when reading from or writing to a destination buffer, which may cause it to access memory that is outside of the bounds of the buffer.

metadata.cwe_name: Buffer Access Using Size of Source Buffer

title: CWE-807: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision
text:
CWE-807 Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision
The product uses a protection mechanism that relies on the existence or values of an input, but the input can be modified by an untrusted actor in a way that bypasses the protection mechanism.

metadata.cwe_name: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision

title: CWE-81: Improper Neutralization of Script in an Error Message Web Page
text:
CWE-81 Improper Neutralization of Script in an Error Message Web Page
The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special characters that could be interpreted as web-scripting elements when they are sent to an error page.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Script in an Error Message Web Page

title: CWE-82: Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes of IMG Tags in a Web Page
text:
CWE-82 Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes of IMG Tags in a Web Page
The web application does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes scripting elements within attributes of HTML IMG tags, such as the src attribute.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes of IMG Tags in a Web Page

title: CWE-820: Missing Synchronization
text:
CWE-820 Missing Synchronization
The product utilizes a shared resource in a concurrent manner but does not attempt to synchronize access to the resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Synchronization

title: CWE-821: Incorrect Synchronization
text:
CWE-821 Incorrect Synchronization
The product utilizes a shared resource in a concurrent manner, but it does not correctly synchronize access to the resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Synchronization

title: CWE-822: Untrusted Pointer Dereference
text:
CWE-822 Untrusted Pointer Dereference
The product obtains a value from an untrusted source, converts this value to a pointer, and dereferences the resulting pointer.

metadata.cwe_name: Untrusted Pointer Dereference

title: CWE-823: Use of Out-of-range Pointer Offset
text:
CWE-823 Use of Out-of-range Pointer Offset
The product performs pointer arithmetic on a valid pointer, but it uses an offset that can point outside of the intended range of valid memory locations for the resulting pointer.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Out-of-range Pointer Offset

title: CWE-824: Access of Uninitialized Pointer
text:
CWE-824 Access of Uninitialized Pointer
The product accesses or uses a pointer that has not been initialized.

metadata.cwe_name: Access of Uninitialized Pointer

title: CWE-825: Expired Pointer Dereference
text:
CWE-825 Expired Pointer Dereference
The product dereferences a pointer that contains a location for memory that was previously valid, but is no longer valid.

metadata.cwe_name: Expired Pointer Dereference

title: CWE-826: Premature Release of Resource During Expected Lifetime
text:
CWE-826 Premature Release of Resource During Expected Lifetime
The product releases a resource that is still intended to be used by itself or another actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Premature Release of Resource During Expected Lifetime

title: CWE-827: Improper Control of Document Type Definition
text:
CWE-827 Improper Control of Document Type Definition
The product does not restrict a reference to a Document Type Definition (DTD) to the intended control sphere. This might allow attackers to reference arbitrary DTDs, possibly causing the product to expose files, consume excessive system resources, or execute arbitrary http requests on behalf of the attacker.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of Document Type Definition

title: CWE-828: Signal Handler with Functionality that is not Asynchronous-Safe
text:
CWE-828 Signal Handler with Functionality that is not Asynchronous-Safe
The product defines a signal handler that contains code sequences that are not asynchronous-safe, i.e., the functionality is not reentrant, or it can be interrupted.

metadata.cwe_name: Signal Handler with Functionality that is not Asynchronous-Safe

title: CWE-829: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
text:
CWE-829 Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
The product imports, requires, or includes executable functionality (such as a library) from a source that is outside of the intended control sphere.

metadata.cwe_name: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere

title: CWE-83: Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes in a Web Page
text:
CWE-83 Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes in a Web Page
The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes "javascript:" or other URIs from dangerous attributes within tags, such as onmouseover, onload, onerror, or style.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes in a Web Page

title: CWE-830: Inclusion of Web Functionality from an Untrusted Source
text:
CWE-830 Inclusion of Web Functionality from an Untrusted Source
The product includes web functionality (such as a web widget) from another domain, which causes it to operate within the domain of the product, potentially granting total access and control of the product to the untrusted source.

metadata.cwe_name: Inclusion of Web Functionality from an Untrusted Source

title: CWE-831: Signal Handler Function Associated with Multiple Signals
text:
CWE-831 Signal Handler Function Associated with Multiple Signals
The product defines a function that is used as a handler for more than one signal.

metadata.cwe_name: Signal Handler Function Associated with Multiple Signals

title: CWE-832: Unlock of a Resource that is not Locked
text:
CWE-832 Unlock of a Resource that is not Locked
The product attempts to unlock a resource that is not locked.

metadata.cwe_name: Unlock of a Resource that is not Locked

title: CWE-833: Deadlock
text:
CWE-833 Deadlock
The product contains multiple threads or executable segments that are waiting for each other to release a necessary lock, resulting in deadlock.

metadata.cwe_name: Deadlock

title: CWE-834: Excessive Iteration
text:
CWE-834 Excessive Iteration
The product performs an iteration or loop without sufficiently limiting the number of times that the loop is executed.

metadata.cwe_name: Excessive Iteration

title: CWE-835: Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')
text:
CWE-835 Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')
The product contains an iteration or loop with an exit condition that cannot be reached, i.e., an infinite loop.

metadata.cwe_name: Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')

title: CWE-836: Use of Password Hash Instead of Password for Authentication
text:
CWE-836 Use of Password Hash Instead of Password for Authentication
The product records password hashes in a data store, receives a hash of a password from a client, and compares the supplied hash to the hash obtained from the data store.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Password Hash Instead of Password for Authentication

title: CWE-837: Improper Enforcement of a Single, Unique Action
text:
CWE-837 Improper Enforcement of a Single, Unique Action
The product requires that an actor should only be able to perform an action once, or to have only one unique action, but the product does not enforce or improperly enforces this restriction.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Enforcement of a Single, Unique Action

title: CWE-838: Inappropriate Encoding for Output Context
text:
CWE-838 Inappropriate Encoding for Output Context
The product uses or specifies an encoding when generating output to a downstream component, but the specified encoding is not the same as the encoding that is expected by the downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Inappropriate Encoding for Output Context

title: CWE-839: Numeric Range Comparison Without Minimum Check
text:
CWE-839 Numeric Range Comparison Without Minimum Check
The product checks a value to ensure that it is less than or equal to a maximum, but it does not also verify that the value is greater than or equal to the minimum.

metadata.cwe_name: Numeric Range Comparison Without Minimum Check

title: CWE-84: Improper Neutralization of Encoded URI Schemes in a Web Page
text:
CWE-84 Improper Neutralization of Encoded URI Schemes in a Web Page
The web application improperly neutralizes user-controlled input for executable script disguised with URI encodings.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Encoded URI Schemes in a Web Page

title: CWE-841: Improper Enforcement of Behavioral Workflow
text:
CWE-841 Improper Enforcement of Behavioral Workflow
The product supports a session in which more than one behavior must be performed by an actor, but it does not properly ensure that the actor performs the behaviors in the required sequence.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Enforcement of Behavioral Workflow

title: CWE-842: Placement of User into Incorrect Group
text:
CWE-842 Placement of User into Incorrect Group
The product or the administrator places a user into an incorrect group.

metadata.cwe_name: Placement of User into Incorrect Group

title: CWE-843: Access of Resource Using Incompatible Type ('Type Confusion')
text:
CWE-843 Access of Resource Using Incompatible Type ('Type Confusion')
The product allocates or initializes a resource such as a pointer, object, or variable using one type, but it later accesses that resource using a type that is incompatible with the original type.

metadata.cwe_name: Access of Resource Using Incompatible Type ('Type Confusion')

title: CWE-85: Doubled Character XSS Manipulations
text:
CWE-85 Doubled Character XSS Manipulations
The web application does not filter user-controlled input for executable script disguised using doubling of the involved characters.

metadata.cwe_name: Doubled Character XSS Manipulations

title: CWE-86: Improper Neutralization of Invalid Characters in Identifiers in Web Pages
text:
CWE-86 Improper Neutralization of Invalid Characters in Identifiers in Web Pages
The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes invalid characters or byte sequences in the middle of tag names, URI schemes, and other identifiers.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Invalid Characters in Identifiers in Web Pages

title: CWE-862: Missing Authorization
text:
CWE-862 Missing Authorization
The product does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Authorization

title: CWE-863: Incorrect Authorization
text:
CWE-863 Incorrect Authorization
The product performs an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action, but it does not correctly perform the check.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrect Authorization

title: CWE-87: Improper Neutralization of Alternate XSS Syntax
text:
CWE-87 Improper Neutralization of Alternate XSS Syntax
The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controlled input for alternate script syntax.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Alternate XSS Syntax

title: CWE-88: Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')
text:
CWE-88 Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')
The product constructs a string for a command to be executed by a separate component
in another control sphere, but it does not properly delimit the
intended arguments, options, or switches within that command string.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')

title: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
text:
CWE-89 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
The product constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended SQL command when it is sent to a downstream component. Without sufficient removal or quoting of SQL syntax in user-controllable inputs, the generated SQL query can cause those inputs to be interpreted as SQL instead of ordinary user data.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

title: CWE-9: J2EE Misconfiguration: Weak Access Permissions for EJB Methods
text:
CWE-9 J2EE Misconfiguration: Weak Access Permissions for EJB Methods
If elevated access rights are assigned to EJB methods, then an attacker can take advantage of the permissions to exploit the product.

metadata.cwe_name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Weak Access Permissions for EJB Methods

title: CWE-90: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')
text:
CWE-90 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')
The product constructs all or part of an LDAP query using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended LDAP query when it is sent to a downstream component.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')

title: CWE-908: Use of Uninitialized Resource
text:
CWE-908 Use of Uninitialized Resource
The product uses or accesses a resource that has not been initialized.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Uninitialized Resource

title: CWE-909: Missing Initialization of Resource
text:
CWE-909 Missing Initialization of Resource
The product does not initialize a critical resource.

metadata.cwe_name: Missing Initialization of Resource

title: CWE-91: XML Injection (aka Blind XPath Injection)
text:
CWE-91 XML Injection (aka Blind XPath Injection)
The product does not properly neutralize special elements that are used in XML, allowing attackers to modify the syntax, content, or commands of the XML before it is processed by an end system.

metadata.cwe_name: XML Injection (aka Blind XPath Injection)

title: CWE-910: Use of Expired File Descriptor
text:
CWE-910 Use of Expired File Descriptor
The product uses or accesses a file descriptor after it has been closed.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Expired File Descriptor

title: CWE-911: Improper Update of Reference Count
text:
CWE-911 Improper Update of Reference Count
The product uses a reference count to manage a resource, but it does not update or incorrectly updates the reference count.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Update of Reference Count

title: CWE-912: Hidden Functionality
text:
CWE-912 Hidden Functionality
The product contains functionality that is not documented, not part of the specification, and not accessible through an interface or command sequence that is obvious to the product's users or administrators.

metadata.cwe_name: Hidden Functionality

title: CWE-913: Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources
text:
CWE-913 Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources
The product does not properly restrict reading from or writing to dynamically-managed code resources such as variables, objects, classes, attributes, functions, or executable instructions or statements.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources

title: CWE-914: Improper Control of Dynamically-Identified Variables
text:
CWE-914 Improper Control of Dynamically-Identified Variables
The product does not properly restrict reading from or writing to dynamically-identified variables.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of Dynamically-Identified Variables

title: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
text:
CWE-915 Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
The product receives input from an upstream component that specifies multiple attributes, properties, or fields that are to be initialized or updated in an object, but it does not properly control which attributes can be modified.

metadata.cwe_name: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes

title: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort
text:
CWE-916 Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort
The product generates a hash for a password, but it uses a scheme that does not provide a sufficient level of computational effort that would make password cracking attacks infeasible or expensive.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

title: CWE-917: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an Expression Language Statement ('Expression Language Injection')
text:
CWE-917 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an Expression Language Statement ('Expression Language Injection')
The product constructs all or part of an expression language (EL) statement in a framework such as a Java Server Page (JSP) using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended EL statement before it is executed.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an Expression Language Statement ('Expression Language Injection')

title: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
text:
CWE-918 Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
The web server receives a URL or similar request from an upstream component and retrieves the contents of this URL, but it does not sufficiently ensure that the request is being sent to the expected destination.

metadata.cwe_name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

title: CWE-92: DEPRECATED: Improper Sanitization of Custom Special Characters
text:
CWE-92 DEPRECATED: Improper Sanitization of Custom Special Characters
This entry has been deprecated. It originally came from PLOVER, which sometimes defined "other" and "miscellaneous" categories in order to satisfy exhaustiveness requirements for taxonomies. Within the context of CWE, the use of a more abstract entry is preferred in mapping situations. CWE-75 is a more appropriate mapping.

metadata.cwe_name: DEPRECATED: Improper Sanitization of Custom Special Characters

title: CWE-920: Improper Restriction of Power Consumption
text:
CWE-920 Improper Restriction of Power Consumption
The product operates in an environment in which power is a limited resource that cannot be automatically replenished, but the product does not properly restrict the amount of power that its operation consumes.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Power Consumption

title: CWE-921: Storage of Sensitive Data in a Mechanism without Access Control
text:
CWE-921 Storage of Sensitive Data in a Mechanism without Access Control
The product stores sensitive information in a file system or device that does not have built-in access control.

metadata.cwe_name: Storage of Sensitive Data in a Mechanism without Access Control

title: CWE-922: Insecure Storage of Sensitive Information
text:
CWE-922 Insecure Storage of Sensitive Information
The product stores sensitive information without properly limiting read or write access by unauthorized actors.

metadata.cwe_name: Insecure Storage of Sensitive Information

title: CWE-923: Improper Restriction of Communication Channel to Intended Endpoints
text:
CWE-923 Improper Restriction of Communication Channel to Intended Endpoints
The product establishes a communication channel to (or from) an endpoint for privileged or protected operations, but it does not properly ensure that it is communicating with the correct endpoint.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Restriction of Communication Channel to Intended Endpoints

title: CWE-924: Improper Enforcement of Message Integrity During Transmission in a Communication Channel
text:
CWE-924 Improper Enforcement of Message Integrity During Transmission in a Communication Channel
The product establishes a communication channel with an endpoint and receives a message from that endpoint, but it does not sufficiently ensure that the message was not modified during transmission.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Enforcement of Message Integrity During Transmission in a Communication Channel

title: CWE-925: Improper Verification of Intent by Broadcast Receiver
text:
CWE-925 Improper Verification of Intent by Broadcast Receiver
The Android application uses a Broadcast Receiver that receives an Intent but does not properly verify that the Intent came from an authorized source.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Verification of Intent by Broadcast Receiver

title: CWE-926: Improper Export of Android Application Components
text:
CWE-926 Improper Export of Android Application Components
The Android application exports a component for use by other applications, but does not properly restrict which applications can launch the component or access the data it contains.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Export of Android Application Components

title: CWE-927: Use of Implicit Intent for Sensitive Communication
text:
CWE-927 Use of Implicit Intent for Sensitive Communication
The Android application uses an implicit intent for transmitting sensitive data to other applications.

metadata.cwe_name: Use of Implicit Intent for Sensitive Communication

title: CWE-93: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences ('CRLF Injection')
text:
CWE-93 Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences ('CRLF Injection')
The product uses CRLF (carriage return line feeds) as a special element, e.g. to separate lines or records, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CRLF sequences from inputs.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences ('CRLF Injection')

title: CWE-939: Improper Authorization in Handler for Custom URL Scheme
text:
CWE-939 Improper Authorization in Handler for Custom URL Scheme
The product uses a handler for a custom URL scheme, but it does not properly restrict which actors can invoke the handler using the scheme.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Authorization in Handler for Custom URL Scheme

title: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
text:
CWE-94 Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
The product constructs all or part of a code segment using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the syntax or behavior of the intended code segment.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

title: CWE-940: Improper Verification of Source of a Communication Channel
text:
CWE-940 Improper Verification of Source of a Communication Channel
The product establishes a communication channel to handle an incoming request that has been initiated by an actor, but it does not properly verify that the request is coming from the expected origin.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Verification of Source of a Communication Channel

title: CWE-941: Incorrectly Specified Destination in a Communication Channel
text:
CWE-941 Incorrectly Specified Destination in a Communication Channel
The product creates a communication channel to initiate an outgoing request to an actor, but it does not correctly specify the intended destination for that actor.

metadata.cwe_name: Incorrectly Specified Destination in a Communication Channel

title: CWE-942: Permissive Cross-domain Security Policy with Untrusted Domains
text:
CWE-942 Permissive Cross-domain Security Policy with Untrusted Domains
The product uses a web-client protection
 mechanism such as a Content Security Policy (CSP) or
 cross-domain policy file, but the policy includes untrusted
 domains with which the web client is allowed to
 communicate.

metadata.cwe_name: Permissive Cross-domain Security Policy with Untrusted Domains

title: CWE-943: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic
text:
CWE-943 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic
The product generates a query intended to access or manipulate data in a data store such as a database, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that can modify the intended logic of the query.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic

title: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')
text:
CWE-95 Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')
The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes code syntax before using the input in a dynamic evaluation call (e.g. "eval").

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

title: CWE-96: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
text:
CWE-96 Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes code syntax before inserting the input into an executable resource, such as a library, configuration file, or template.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')

title: CWE-97: Improper Neutralization of Server-Side Includes (SSI) Within a Web Page
text:
CWE-97 Improper Neutralization of Server-Side Includes (SSI) Within a Web Page
The product generates a web page, but does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input that could be interpreted as a server-side include (SSI) directive.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Neutralization of Server-Side Includes (SSI) Within a Web Page

title: CWE-98: Improper Control of Filename for Include/Require Statement in PHP Program ('PHP Remote File Inclusion')
text:
CWE-98 Improper Control of Filename for Include/Require Statement in PHP Program ('PHP Remote File Inclusion')
The PHP application receives input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the input before its usage in "require," "include," or similar functions.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of Filename for Include/Require Statement in PHP Program ('PHP Remote File Inclusion')

title: CWE-99: Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
text:
CWE-99 Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the input before it is used as an identifier for a resource that may be outside the intended sphere of control.

metadata.cwe_name: Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
